name: gluoncv
documentation:
# GluonCV Python Package

[GluonCV](https://gluon-cv.mxnet.io) provides implementations of the state-of-the-art (SOTA) deep learning models in computer vision.

It is designed for engineers, researchers, and students to fast prototype products and research ideas based on these models.

## Installation

To install, use:

```bash
pip install gluoncv mxnet>=1.6.0 --upgrade
# for installing gluoncv with all dependencies
pip install gluoncv[full] mxnet>=1.6.0 --upgrade
```

To enable different hardware supports such as GPUs, check out [mxnet variants](https://pypi.org/project/mxnet/).

For example, you can install cuda-11.0 supported mxnet alongside gluoncv:

```bash
pip install gluoncv mxnet-cu110>=1.6.0 --upgrade
```

functions.name: tests.unittests.common.random_seed
documentation:
Runs a code block with a new seed for np, mx and python's random.

Parameters
----------

seed : the seed to pass to np.random, mx.random and python's random.

To impose rng determinism, invoke e.g. as in:

with random_seed(1234):
    ...

To impose rng non-determinism, invoke as in:

with random_seed():
    ...

Upon conclusion of the block, the rng's are returned to
a state that is a function of their pre-block state, so
any prior non-determinism is preserved.

functions.name: tests.unittests.common.with_seed
documentation:
A decorator for nosetests test functions that manages rng seeds.

Parameters
----------

seed : the seed to pass to np.random and mx.random


This tests decorator sets the np, mx and python random seeds identically
prior to each test, then outputs those seeds if the test fails or
if the test requires a fixed seed (as a reminder to make the test
more robust against random data).

@with_seed()
def test_ok_with_random_data():
    ...

@with_seed(1234)
def test_not_ok_with_random_data():
    ...

Use of the @with_seed() decorator for all tests creates
tests isolation and reproducibility of failures.  When a
test fails, the decorator outputs the seed used.  The user
can then set the environment variable MXNET_TEST_SEED to
the value reported, then rerun the test with:

    nosetests --verbose -s <test_module_name.py>:<failing_test>

To run a test repeatedly, set MXNET_TEST_COUNT=<NNN> in the environment.
To see the seeds of even the passing tests, add '--logging-level=DEBUG' to nosetests.

functions.name: tests.unittests.common.setup_module
documentation:
A function with a 'magic name' executed automatically before each nosetests module
(file of tests) that helps reproduce a test segfault by setting and outputting the rng seeds.

The segfault-debug procedure on a module called test_module.py is:

1. run "nosetests --verbose test_module.py".  A seg-faulting output might be:

   [INFO] np, mx and python random seeds = 4018804151
   test_module.test1 ... ok
   test_module.test2 ... Illegal instruction (core dumped)

2. Copy the module-starting seed into the next command, then run:

   MXNET_MODULE_SEED=4018804151 nosetests --logging-level=DEBUG --verbose test_module.py

   Output might be:

   [WARNING] **** module-level seed is set: all tests running deterministically ****
   [INFO] np, mx and python random seeds = 4018804151
   test_module.test1 ... [DEBUG] np and mx random seeds = 3935862516
   ok
   test_module.test2 ... [DEBUG] np and mx random seeds = 1435005594
   Illegal instruction (core dumped)

3. Copy the segfaulting-test seed into the command:
   MXNET_TEST_SEED=1435005594 nosetests --logging-level=DEBUG --verbose test_module.py:test2
   Output might be:

   [INFO] np, mx and python random seeds = 2481884723
   test_module.test2 ... [DEBUG] np and mx random seeds = 1435005594
   Illegal instruction (core dumped)

3. Finally reproduce the segfault directly under gdb (might need additional os packages)
   by editing the bottom of test_module.py to be

   if __name__ == '__main__':
       logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
       test2()

   MXNET_TEST_SEED=1435005594 gdb -ex r --args python test_module.py

4. When finished debugging the segfault, remember to unset any exported MXNET_ seed
   variables in the environment to return to non-deterministic testing (a good thing).

functions.name: tests.model_zoo_torch.test_tvm_torch_export.test_model_zoo_tvm_export_directpose
documentation: def test_model_zoo_tvm_export():
from tvm.contrib.download import download_testdata
img_url = "https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet.js/blob/main/data/cat.png?raw=true"
img_path = download_testdata(img_url, "cat.png", module="data")
img = Image.open(img_path).resize((224, 224))
my_preprocess = transforms.Compose(
transforms.Resize(256),
transforms.CenterCrop(224),
transforms.ToTensor(),
img = my_preprocess(img)
img = np.expand_dims(img, 0)
model = getattr(torchvision.models, "resnet18")(pretrained=True)
_test_tvm_export(model, img)

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.model_zoo.get_model
documentation:
Returns a pre-defined model by name

Parameters
----------
name : str
    Name of the model.
pretrained : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
classes : int
    Number of classes for the output layer.
ctx : Context, default CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.

Returns
-------
HybridBlock
    The model.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.anchor.SSDAnchorGenerator.hybrid_forward
documentation: pylint: disable=arguments-differ

Bounding box anchor generator for Single-shot Object Detection.

Parameters
----------
index : int
    Index of this generator in SSD models, this is required for naming.
sizes : iterable of floats
    Sizes of anchor boxes.
ratios : iterable of floats
    Aspect ratios of anchor boxes.
step : int or float
    Step size of anchor boxes.
alloc_size : tuple of int
    Allocate size for the anchor boxes as (H, W).
    Usually we generate enough anchors for large feature map, e.g. 128x128.
    Later in inference we can have variable input sizes,
    at which time we can crop corresponding anchors from this large
    anchor map so we can skip re-generating anchors for each input.
offsets : tuple of float
    Center offsets of anchor boxes as (h, w) in range(0, 1).

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.anchor.LiteAnchorGenerator.num_depth
documentation: Number of anchors at each pixel.

Bounding box anchor generator for Single-shot Object Detection, corresponding to anchors
structure used in ssd_mobilenet_v1_coco from TF Object Detection API
This class inherits SSDAnchorGenerator and uses the same input parameters.
Main differences:
  - First branch is not added with another anchor with size extracted from
    the geomtric mean of current and next branch sizes.
  - First anchor in the first branch has half the size of the rest of the anchors.
  - Geometric sum anchors are added to all other branches as the last anchor.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.resnet_v1b_ssd.ResNetV1bSSD
documentation:
Single-shot Object Detection Network: <https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.02325>.

: with resnetv1b base model.

# Parameters

network

: Name of the base network, must be end with v1b.

add_filters

: Number of channels for the appended layers, ignored if `` network`is `None ``.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.
  This will only apply to base networks that has `norm_layer` specified, will ignore if the
  base network (e.g. VGG) don't accept this argument.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

use_bn

: Whether to use BatchNorm layer after each attached convolutional layer.

reduce_ratio

: Channel reduce ratio (0, 1) of the transition layer.

min_depth

: Minimum channels for the transition layers.

Training targets generator for Single-shot Object Detection.

stds : array-like of size 4, default is (0.1, 0.1, 0.2, 0.2)

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.hrnet.get_hrnet
documentation:
HRNet model from the
["Deep High-Resolution Representation Learning for Visual Recognition"](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1908.07919) paper.

# Parameters

model_name

: The name of hrnet models: w18_small_v1/w18_small_v2/w30/w32/w40/w42/w48.

stage_interp_type

: The interpolation type for upsample in each stage, nearest, bilinear and
  bilinear_like are supported.

purpose: string

: The purpose of model, cls and seg are supported.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.
pylint: enable=C0326

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.hrnet.OrigHRBottleneck
documentation:
Modified Bottleneck V1 from ["Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition"](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385) paper.
This is used for ResNet V1 for 50, 101, 152 layers. Its all convolutions are
no bias to match with the original hrnet implementation.

# Parameters

channels

: Number of output channels.

stride

: Stride size.

downsample

: Whether to downsample the input.

in_channels

: Number of input channels. Default is 0, to infer from the graph.

last_gamma

: Whether to initialize the gamma of the last BatchNorm layer in each bottleneck to zero.

use_se

: Whether to use Squeeze-and-Excitation module

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

modules.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.ssd
documentation: Single-shot Multi-box Detector.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.ssd.get_ssd
documentation:
Get SSD models.

# Parameters

name

: Model name, if `None` is used, you must specify `features` to be a `HybridBlock`.

base_size

: Base image size for training, this is fixed once training is assigned.
  A fixed base size still allows you to have variable input size during test.

features

: List of network internal output names, in order to specify which layers are
  used for predicting bbox values.
  If `name` is `None`, `features` must be a `HybridBlock` which generate multiple
  outputs for prediction.

filters

: List of convolution layer channels which is going to be appended to the base
  network feature extractor. If `name` is `None`, this is ignored.

sizes

: Sizes of anchor boxes, this should be a list of floats, in incremental order.
  The length of `sizes` must be len(layers) + 1. For example, a two stage SSD
  model can have `sizes = [30, 60, 90]`, and it converts to `[30, 60]` and
  `[60, 90]` for the two stages, respectively. For more details, please refer
  to original paper.

ratios

: Aspect ratios of anchors in each output layer. Its length must be equals
  to the number of SSD output layers.

steps

: Step size of anchor boxes in each output layer.

classes

: Names of categories.

dataset

: Name of dataset. This is used to identify model name because models trained on
  different datasets are going to be very different.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

ctx

: Context such as mx.cpu(), mx.gpu(0).

root

: Model weights storing path.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A SSD detection network.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.ssd.SSD.set_nms
documentation:
Set non-maximum suppression parameters.

Parameters
----------
nms_thresh : float, default is 0.45.
    Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.
nms_topk : int, default is 400
    Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
     result is used in NMS.
post_nms : int, default is 100
    Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
    based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
    number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

Returns
-------
None

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.ssd.SSD.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.

# Parameters

classes

: The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.

reuse_weights

: A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
  or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
  This allows the new predictor to reuse the
  previously trained weights specified.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = gluoncv.model_zoo.get_model('ssd_512_resnet50_v1_voc', pretrained=True)
>>> # use direct name to name mapping to reuse weights
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':'person'})
>>> # or use interger mapping, person is the 14th category in VOC
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={0:14})
>>> # you can even mix them
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':14})
>>> # or use a list of string if class name don't change
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights=['person'])
```

Single-shot Object Detection Network: <https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.02325>.

# Parameters

network

: Name of the base network, if `None` is used, will instantiate the
  base network from `features` directly instead of composing.

base_size

: Base input size, it is speficied so SSD can support dynamic input shapes.

features

: Intermediate features to be extracted or a network with multi-output.
  If `network` is `None`, `features` is expected to be a multi-output network.

num_filters

: Number of channels for the appended layers, ignored if `` network`is `None ``.

sizes

: Sizes of anchor boxes, this should be a list of floats, in incremental order.
  The length of `sizes` must be len(layers) + 1. For example, a two stage SSD
  model can have `sizes = [30, 60, 90]`, and it converts to `[30, 60]` and
  `[60, 90]` for the two stages, respectively. For more details, please refer
  to original paper.

ratios

: Aspect ratios of anchors in each output layer. Its length must be equals
  to the number of SSD output layers.

steps

: Step size of anchor boxes in each output layer.

classes

: Names of all categories.

use_1x1_transition

: Whether to use 1x1 convolution as transition layer between attached layers,
  it is effective reducing model capacity.

use_bn

: Whether to use BatchNorm layer after each attached convolutional layer.

reduce_ratio

: Channel reduce ratio (0, 1) of the transition layer.

min_depth

: Minimum channels for the transition layers.

global_pool

: Whether to attach a global average pooling layer as the last output layer.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

stds

: Std values to be divided/multiplied to box encoded values.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

post_nms

: Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
  based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
  number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

anchor_alloc_size

: For advanced users. Define `anchor_alloc_size` to generate large enough anchor
  maps, which will later saved in parameters. During inference, we support arbitrary
  input image by cropping corresponding area of the anchor map. This allow us
  to export to symbol so we can run it in c++, scalar, etc.

ctx

: Network context.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.
  This will only apply to base networks that has `norm_layer` specified, will ignore if the
  base network (e.g. VGG) don't accept this argument.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

root

: The root path for model storage, default is '~/.mxnet/models'

minimal_opset

: We sometimes add special operators to accelerate training/inference, however, for exporting
  to third party compilers we want to utilize most widely used operators.
  If `minimal_opset` is `True`, the network will use a minimal set of operators good
  for e.g., `TVM`.

predictor_kernel: tuple of int. default is (3,3)

: Dimension of predictor kernel

predictor_pad: tuple of int. default is (1,1)

: Padding of the predictor kenrel conv.

anchor_generator: default is SSDAnchorGenerator

: Anchor Generator to be used. The default it SSDAnchorGenerator corresponding
  to SSD published article. This argument can be used for other custom
  anchor generators. Like LiteAnchorGenerator.

documentation:
Densenet-BC model from the
["Densely Connected Convolutional Networks"](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1608.06993.pdf) paper.

# Parameters

num_init_features

: Number of filters to learn in the first convolution layer.

growth_rate

: Number of filters to add each layer (`k` in the paper).

block_config

: List of integers for numbers of layers in each pooling block.

bn_size

: Multiplicative factor for number of bottle neck layers.
  (i.e. bn_size * k features in the bottleneck layer)

dropout

: Rate of dropout after each dense layer.

classes

: Number of classification classes.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

SSD architecture with VGG16 atrous 300x300 base network for COCO.

# Parameters

classes

: Names of custom foreground classes. `len(classes)` is the number of foreground classes.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized.

transfer

: If not `None`, will try to reuse pre-trained weights from SSD networks trained on other
  datasets.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A SSD detection network.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = ssd_300_vgg16_atrous_custom(classes=['a', 'b', 'c'], pretrained_base=True)
>>> net = ssd_300_vgg16_atrous_custom(classes=['foo', 'bar'], transfer='coco')
```

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A SSD detection network.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.ssd.presets.ssd_512_resnet50_v1_custom
documentation:
SSD architecture with ResNet50 v1 512 base network for custom dataset.

# Parameters

classes

: Names of custom foreground classes. `len(classes)` is the number of foreground classes.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized.

transfer

: If not `None`, will try to reuse pre-trained weights from SSD networks trained on other
  datasets.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A SSD detection network.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = ssd_512_resnet50_v1_custom(classes=['a', 'b', 'c'], pretrained_base=True)
>>> net = ssd_512_resnet50_v1_custom(classes=['foo', 'bar'], transfer='voc')
```

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.pruned_resnet.resnetv1b_pruned.prune_gluon_block
documentation:
---
param net: original network that is required to be pruned
param params: dictionary of parameters for the pruned network. Size of the parameters
  in
param params_shapes: dictionary of shapes of convolutional weights
param prefix: prefix of the original resnet50_v1d
param pretrained: Boolean specifying if the pretrained model parameters needs to be
  loaded
---

this dictionary tells what
should be the size of channels of each convolution layer.
\:param ctx: cpu(0)
\:return: "net"

ctx : Context, default CPU

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.nasnet.get_nasnet
documentation:
NASNet A model from
["Learning Transferable Architectures for Scalable Image Recognition"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1707.07012) paper

# Parameters

repeat

: Number of cell repeats

penultimate_filters

: Number of filters in the penultimate layer of the network

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.resnest.ResNeSt.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=unused-variable

ResNeSt Model
Parameters
\----------
block : Block

> Class for the residual block. Options are BasicBlockV1, BottleneckV1.

layers

: Numbers of layers in each block

classes

: Number of classification classes.

dilated

: Applying dilation strategy to pretrained ResNet yielding a stride-8 model,
  typically used in Semantic Segmentation.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

last_gamma

: Whether to initialize the gamma of the last BatchNorm layer in each bottleneck to zero.

deep_stem

: Whether to replace the 7x7 conv1 with 3 3x3 convolution layers.

avg_down

: Whether to use average pooling for projection skip connection between stages/downsample.

final_drop

: Dropout ratio before the final classification layer.

use_global_stats

: Whether forcing BatchNorm to use global statistics instead of minibatch statistics;
  optionally set to True if finetuning using ImageNet classification pretrained models.

Reference:

: - He, Kaiming, et al. "Deep residual learning for image recognition."

  Proceedings of the IEEE conference on computer vision and pattern recognition. 2016.
  \- Yu, Fisher, and Vladlen Koltun. "Multi-scale context aggregation by dilated convolutions."

documentation:
Generate heatmap target and target_weight

Parameters
----------
joints_3d : numpy.ndarray
    3D joints, with shape (num_joints, 3, 2)

Returns
-------
(numpy.ndarray, numpy.ndarray)
    target : regression target, with shape (num_joints, heatmap_h, heatmap_w)
    target_weight : target weight to mask out non-interest target, shape (num_joints, 1, 1)

Gaussian heatmap target generator for simple pose.
Adapted from https://github.com/Microsoft/human-pose-estimation.pytorch

Parameters
----------
num_joints : int
    Number of joints defined by dataset
image_size : tuple of int
    Image size, as (width, height).
heatmap_size : tuple of int
    Heatmap size, as (width, height).
sigma : float
    Gaussian sigma for the heatmap generation.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_inceptionv3.i3d_inceptionv3_kinetics400
documentation:
Inception v3 model trained on Kinetics400 dataset from
`"Rethinking the Inception Architecture for Computer Vision"
<http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.00567>`_ paper.

Inflated 3D model (I3D) from
`"Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset"
<https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.07750>`_ paper.

Parameters
----------
nclass : int.
    Number of categories in the dataset.
pretrained : bool or str.
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
pretrained_base : bool or str, optional, default is True.
    Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
    if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.
ctx : Context, default CPU.
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default $MXNET_HOME/models
    Location for keeping the model parameters.
num_segments : int, default is 1.
    Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.
num_crop : int, default is 1.
    Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.
partial_bn : bool, default False.
    Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.
feat_ext : bool.
    Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
    do a complete forward pass.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_inceptionv3.I3D_InceptionV3
documentation:
Inception v3 model from
["Rethinking the Inception Architecture for Computer Vision"](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.00567) paper.

Inflated 3D model (I3D) from
["Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.07750) paper.

This model definition file is written by Brais and modified by Yi.

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of classes in the training dataset.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

dropout_ratio

: The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
  The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.

num_segments

: Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.

num_crop

: Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

feat_ext

: Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
  do a complete forward pass.

init_std

: Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.actionrec_inceptionv1.ActionRecInceptionV1
documentation:
Inception v1 model for video action recognition
Christian Szegedy, etal, Going Deeper with Convolutions, CVPR 2015
https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.4842
Limin Wang, etal, Towards Good Practices for Very Deep Two-Stream ConvNets, arXiv 2015
https://arxiv.org/abs/1507.02159
Limin Wang, etal, Temporal Segment Networks: Towards Good Practices for Deep Action Recognition, ECCV 2016
https://arxiv.org/abs/1608.00859

Parameters
----------
nclass : int
    Number of classes in the training dataset.
pretrained_base : bool or str, optional, default is True.
    Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
    if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.
partial_bn : bool, default False.
    Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.
dropout_ratio : float, default is 0.5.
    The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
    The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.
init_std : float, default is 0.001.
    Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.
num_segments : int, default is 1.
    Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.
num_crop : int, default is 1.
    Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

Input: a single video frame or N images from N segments when num_segments > 1
Output: a single predicted action label

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.slowfast.Bottleneck

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.slowfast.Bottleneck.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of a ResNet bottleneck block

Bottleneck building block for ResNet50, ResNet101 and ResNet152.

# Parameters

inplanes

: Input channels of each block.

planes

: Output channels of each block.

strides

: Stride in convolution layers.

head_conv

: Determin whether we do 1x1x1 convolution or 3x1x1 convolution.

downsample

: Whether to contain a downsampling layer in the block.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

layer_name

: Give a name to current block.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.slowfast.SlowFast._make_layer_fast
documentation: Build each stage of within the fast branch.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.slowfast.SlowFast._make_layer_slow
documentation: Build each stage of within the slow branch.

SlowFast networks (SlowFast) from
["SlowFast Networks for Video Recognition"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1812.03982) paper.

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of categories in the dataset.

block

: Building block of a ResNet, could be Basic or Bottleneck.

layers

: Number of stages in a ResNet, e.g., [3, 4, 6, 3] in ResNet50.

num_block_temp_kernel_fast

: If the current block has more than NUM_BLOCK_TEMP_KERNEL blocks,
  use temporal kernel of 1 for the rest of the blocks.

num_block_temp_kernel_slow

: If the current block has more than NUM_BLOCK_TEMP_KERNEL blocks,
  use temporal kernel of 1 for the rest of the blocks.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

feat_ext

: Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
  do a complete forward pass.

num_segments

: Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.

num_crop

: Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

bn_eval

: Whether to set BN layers to eval mode, namely, freeze
  running stats (mean and var).

bn_frozen

: Whether to freeze weight and bias of BN layers.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

frozen_stages

: Stages to be frozen (all param fixed). -1 means not freezing any parameters.

dropout_ratio

: The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
  The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.

init_std

: Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.

alpha

: Corresponds to the frame rate reduction ratio between the Slow and Fast pathways.

beta_inv

: Corresponds to the inverse of the channel reduction ratio between the Slow and Fast pathways.

fusion_conv_channel_ratio

: Ratio of channel dimensions between the Slow and Fast pathways.

fusion_kernel_size

: Kernel dimension used for fusing information from Fast pathway to Slow pathway.

width_per_group

: Width of each group (64 -> ResNet; 4 -> ResNeXt).

num_groups

: Number of groups for the convolution.
  Num_groups=1 is for standard ResNet like networks,
  and num_groups>1 is for ResNeXt like networks.

slow_temporal_stride

: The temporal stride for sparse sampling of video frames in slow branch of a SlowFast network.

fast_temporal_stride

: The temporal stride for sparse sampling of video frames in fast branch of a SlowFast network.

slow_frames

: The number of frames used as input to a slow branch.

fast_frames

: The number of frames used as input to a fast branch.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.actionrec_vgg16.ActionRecVGG16
documentation:
VGG16 model for video action recognition
Karen Simonyan and Andrew Zisserman, Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition, arXiv 2014
https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.1556
Limin Wang, etal, Towards Good Practices for Very Deep Two-Stream ConvNets, arXiv 2015
https://arxiv.org/abs/1507.02159
Limin Wang, etal, Temporal Segment Networks: Towards Good Practices for Deep Action Recognition, ECCV 2016
https://arxiv.org/abs/1608.00859

Parameters
----------
nclass : int
    Number of classes in the training dataset.
pretrained_base : bool or str, optional, default is True.
    Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
    if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.
dropout_ratio : float, default is 0.5.
    The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
    The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.
init_std : float, default is 0.001.
    Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.
num_segments : int, default is 1.
    Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.
num_crop : int, default is 1.
    Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

Input: a single video frame or N images from N segments when num_segments > 1
Output: a single predicted action label

root : str, default $MXNET_HOME/models

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_inceptionv1.I3D_InceptionV1
documentation:
Inception v1 model from
["Going Deeper with Convolutions"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.4842) paper.

Inflated 3D model (I3D) from
["Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.07750) paper.
Slight differences between this implementation and the original implementation due to padding.

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of classes in the training dataset.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

dropout_ratio

: The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
  The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.

num_segments

: Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.

num_crop

: Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

feat_ext

: Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
  do a complete forward pass.

init_std

: Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_slow.ResNet_SlowFast.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of I3D_slow network

ResNe(x)t_SlowFast backbone.
Args:
    depth (int): Depth of resnet, from {50, 101, 152}.
    num_stages (int): Resnet stages, normally 4.
    strides (Sequence[int]): Strides of the first block of each stage.
    dilations (Sequence[int]): Dilation of each stage.
    out_indices (Sequence[int]): Output from which stages.
    frozen_stages (int): Stages to be frozen (all param fixed). -1 means
        not freezing any parameters.
    bn_eval (bool): Whether to set BN layers to eval mode, namely, freeze
        running stats (mean and var).
    bn_frozen (bool): Whether to freeze weight and bias of BN layers.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.p3d.Bottleneck.P3DA
documentation:
P3D-A unit
Stacked architecture by making temporal 1D filters (T)
follow spatial 2D filters (S) in a cascaded manner.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.p3d.Bottleneck.P3DB
documentation:
P3D-B unit
Parallel architecture by making temporal 1D filters (T)
and spatial 2D filters (S) in different pathways.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.p3d.Bottleneck.P3DC
documentation:
P3D-C unit
A compromise design between P3D-A and P3D-B.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.p3d.Bottleneck.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of a ResBlock in P3D.

ResBlock for P3D

# Parameters

inplanes

: Input channels of each block.

planes

: Output channels of each block.

spatial_stride

: Stride in spatial dimension of convolutional layers in a block.

temporal_stride

: Stride in temporal dimension of convolutional layers in a block.

dilation

: Dilation of convolutional layers in a block.

downsample

: Whether to contain a downsampling layer in the block.

num_layers

: The current depth of this layer.

depth_3d

: Number of 3D layers in the network. For example,
  a P3D with ResNet50 backbone has a depth_3d of 13, which is the sum of 3, 4 and 6.

block_design

: Different designs for each block, from 'A', 'B' or 'C'.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

layer_name

: Give a name to current block.

# Parameters

block

: Class for the residual block.

layers

: Numbers of layers in each block

block_design

: Different designs for each block, from 'A', 'B' or 'C'.

dropout_ratio

: The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
  The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.

num_segments

: Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.

num_crop

: Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

feat_ext

: Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
  do a complete forward pass.

init_std

: Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.actionrec_resnetv1b.ActionRecResNetV1b
documentation:
ResNet models for video action recognition
Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition, CVPR 2016
https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385

Parameters
----------
depth : int, default is 50.
    Depth of ResNet, from {18, 34, 50, 101, 152}.
nclass : int
    Number of classes in the training dataset.
pretrained_base : bool or str, optional, default is True.
    Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
    if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.
partial_bn : bool, default False.
    Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.
dropout_ratio : float, default is 0.5.
    The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
    The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.
init_std : float, default is 0.001.
    Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.
num_segments : int, default is 1.
    Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.
num_crop : int, default is 1.
    Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

Input: a single video frame or N images from N segments when num_segments > 1
Output: a single predicted action label

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.r2plus1d.Conv2Plus1D.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of a Conv2Plus1D block.

Building block of Conv2Plus1D

# Parameters

inplanes

: Input channels of each block.

planes

: Output channels of each block.

midplanes

: Intermediate channels of each block.

stride

: Stride in each dimension of 3D convolutional layers in a block.

padding

: Padding in each dimension of the feature map.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.r2plus1d.BasicBlock

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.r2plus1d.BasicBlock.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of a ResBlock in R2+1D.

ResNet Basic Block for R2Plus1D

# Parameters

inplanes

: Input channels of each block.

planes

: Output channels of each block.

stride

: Stride in each dimension of 3D convolutional layers in a block.

downsample

: Whether to contain a downsampling layer in the block.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

layer_name

: Give a name to current block.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.r2plus1d.R2Plus1D._make_res_layer
documentation: Build each stage of a ResNet

The R2+1D network.
A Closer Look at Spatiotemporal Convolutions for Action Recognition.
CVPR, 2018. <https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.11248>

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of classes in the training dataset.

block

: Class for the residual block.

layers

: Numbers of layers in each block

dropout_ratio

: The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
  The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.

num_segments

: Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.

num_crop

: Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

feat_ext

: Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
  do a complete forward pass.

init_std

: Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

Not supported for I3D at this moment.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_resnet.Bottleneck

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_resnet.Bottleneck.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of a ResNet bottleneck block

Bottleneck building block for ResNet50, ResNet101 and ResNet152.

# Parameters

inplanes

: Input channels of each block.

planes

: Output channels of each block.

spatial_stride

: Stride in spatial dimension of convolutional layers in a block.

temporal_stride

: Stride in temporal dimension of convolutional layers in a block.

dilation

: Dilation of convolutional layers in a block.

downsample

: Whether to contain a downsampling layer in the block.

if_inflate

: Whether enable inflation of 3D convolutional layers in this block.

inflate_style

: How to inflate a 2D kernel, either '3x1x1' or '1x3x3'.

if_nonlocal

: Whether to insert a non-local block after this ResNet block.

nonlocal_cfg

: Additional `non-local` arguments, for example `nonlocal_type='gaussian'`.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

layer_name

: Give a name to current block.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_resnet.I3D_ResNetV1

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_resnet.I3D_ResNetV1.init_weights
documentation: Initial I3D network with its 2D pretrained weights.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.action_recognition.i3d_resnet.I3D_ResNetV1.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of I3D network

ResNet_I3D backbone.
Inflated 3D model (I3D) from
["Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.07750) paper.

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of categories in the dataset.

depth

: Depth of ResNet, from {18, 34, 50, 101, 152}.

num_stages

: Number of stages in a ResNet.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

feat_ext

: Whether to extract features before dense classification layer or
  do a complete forward pass.

num_segments

: Number of segments used to evenly divide a video.

num_crop

: Number of crops used during evaluation, choices are 1, 3 or 10.

spatial_strides

: Strides in the spatial dimension of the first block of each stage.

temporal_strides

: Strides in the temporal dimension of the first block of each stage.

dilations

: Dilation ratio of each stage.

out_indices

: Collect features from the selected stages of ResNet,
  usually used for feature extraction or auxililary loss.

conv1_kernel_t

: The kernel size of first convolutional layer in a ResNet.

conv1_stride_t

: The stride of first convolutional layer in a ResNet.

pool1_kernel_t

: The kernel size of first pooling layer in a ResNet.

pool1_stride_t

: The stride of first pooling layer in a ResNet.

inflate_freq

: Select which 2D convolutional layers to be inflated to 3D convolutional layers in each stage.

inflate_stride

: The stride for inflated layers in each stage.

inflate_style

: How to inflate a 2D kernel, either '3x1x1' or '1x3x3'.

nonlocal_stages

: Select which stage we need non-local blocks.

nonlocal_freq

: Select where to insert non-local blocks in each stage.

nonlocal_cfg

: Additional `non-local` arguments, for example `nonlocal_type='gaussian'`.

bn_eval

: Whether to set BN layers to eval mode, namely, freeze
  running stats (mean and var).

bn_frozen

: Whether to freeze weight and bias of BN layers.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

frozen_stages

: Stages to be frozen (all param fixed). -1 means not freezing any parameters.

dropout_ratio

: The dropout rate of a dropout layer.
  The larger the value, the more strength to prevent overfitting.

init_std

: Standard deviation value when initialize the dense layers.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

documentation:
MobileNet model from the
["MobileNets: Efficient Convolutional Neural Networks for Mobile Vision Applications"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1704.04861) paper.

# Parameters

multiplier

: The width multiplier for controlling the model size. Only multipliers that are no
  less than 0.25 are supported. The actual number of channels is equal to the original
  channel size multiplied by this multiplier.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.
Constructor

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.pspnet.get_psp
documentation:
Pyramid Scene Parsing Network
Parameters
\----------
dataset : str, default pascal_voc

> The dataset that model pretrained on. (pascal_voc, ade20k)

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

pretrained_base

: This will load pretrained backbone network, that was trained on ImageNet.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = get_fcn(dataset='pascal_voc', backbone='resnet50', pretrained=False)
>>> print(model)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.pspnet.PSPNet
documentation:
Pyramid Scene Parsing Network

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of categories for the training dataset.

backbone

: Pre-trained dilated backbone network type (default:'resnet50'; 'resnet50',
  'resnet101' or 'resnet152').

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used in backbone network (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`;
  for Synchronized Cross-GPU BachNormalization).

aux

: Auxiliary loss.

Reference:

> Zhao, Hengshuang, Jianping Shi, Xiaojuan Qi, Xiaogang Wang, and Jiaya Jia.
> "Pyramid scene parsing network." *CVPR*, 2017

documentation:
inputs :
    x : input feature maps( B X C X H X W)
returns :
    out : attention value + input feature

Position attention module
from the paper `"Dual Attention Network for Scene Segmentation"
<https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.02983>`
PAM_Module captures long-range spatial contextual information.

Channel attention module
from the paper `"Dual Attention Network for Scene Segmentation"
<https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.02983>`
CAM_Module explicitly models interdependencies between channels.

documentation:
Image Cascade Network (ICNet)
ICNet for Real-Time Semantic Segmentation on High-Resolution Images, ECCV 2018
https://hszhao.github.io/projects/icnet/
Code partially borrowed from https://github.com/lxtGH/Fast_Seg/blob/master/libs/models/ICNet.py.

    Pre-trained dilated backbone network type (default:'resnet50'; 'resnet50',
    'resnet101' or 'resnet152').
pretrained : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

    Location for keeping the model parameters.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.icnet.ICNet
documentation:
Image Cascade Network (ICNet)

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of categories for the training dataset.

backbone

: Pre-trained dilated backbone network type (default:'resnet50'; 'resnet50',
  'resnet101' or 'resnet152').

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used in backbone network (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`;
  for Synchronized Cross-GPU BachNormalization).

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

pretrained_base

: Refers to if the backbone is pretrained or not. If `True`,
  model weights of a model that was trained on ImageNet is loaded.

Reference:

> Hengshuang Zhao, Xiaojuan Qi, Xiaoyong Shen, Jianping Shi and Jiaya Jia.
> "ICNet for Real-Time Semantic Segmentation on High-Resolution Images." *ECCV*, 2018

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = ICNet(nclass=19, backbone='resnet50')
>>> print(model)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.center_net.deconv_resnet.BilinearUpSample
documentation:
Initializes weights as bilinear upsampling kernel.

# Example

```pycon
>>> # Given 'module', an instance of 'mxnet.module.Module',
initialize weights to bilinear upsample...
>>> init = mx.initializer.BilinearUpSample()
>>> module.init_params(init)
>>> for dictionary in module.get_params():
...     for key in dictionary:
...         print(key)
...         print(dictionary[key].asnumpy())
...
fullyconnected0_weight
[[ 0.  0.  0.]]
```

pretrained_base : bool
    Whether load pretrained base network.
norm_layer : mxnet.gluon.nn.HybridBlock
    Type of Norm layers, can be BatchNorm, SyncBatchNorm, GroupNorm, etc.
norm_kwargs : dict
    Additional kwargs for `norm_layer`.
use_dcnv2 : bool
    If true, will use DCNv2 layers in upsampling blocks

Return number of foreground classes.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.center_net.center_net.CenterNet.set_nms
documentation:
Set non-maximum suppression parameters.

Parameters
----------
nms_thresh : float, default is 0.
    Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.
    By default NMS is disabled.
nms_topk : int, default is 400
    Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
     result is used in NMS.
post_nms : int, default is 100
    Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
    based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
    number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

Returns
-------
None

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.center_net.center_net.CenterNet.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.

# Parameters

classes

: The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.

reuse_weights

: A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
  or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
  This allows the new predictor to reuse the
  previously trained weights specified.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = gluoncv.model_zoo.get_model('center_net_resnet50_v1b_voc', pretrained=True)
>>> # use direct name to name mapping to reuse weights
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':'person'})
>>> # or use interger mapping, person is the 14th category in VOC
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={0:14})
>>> # you can even mix them
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':14})
>>> # or use a list of string if class name don't change
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights=['person'])
```

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.center_net.center_net.CenterNet.hybrid_forward
documentation: Hybrid forward of center net

Objects as Points. https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.07850v2

Parameters
----------
base_network : mxnet.gluon.nn.HybridBlock
    The base feature extraction network.
heads : OrderedDict
    OrderedDict with specifications for each head.
    For example: OrderedDict([
        ('heatmap', {'num_output': len(classes), 'bias': -2.19}),
        ('wh', {'num_output': 2}),
        ('reg', {'num_output': 2})
        ])
classes : list of str
    Category names.
head_conv_channel : int, default is 0
    If > 0, will use an extra conv layer before each of the real heads.
scale : float, default is 4.0
    The downsampling ratio of the entire network.
topk : int, default is 100
    Number of outputs .
flip_test : bool
    Whether apply flip test in inference (training mode not affected).
nms_thresh : float, default is 0.
    Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.
    By default nms is disabled.
nms_topk : int, default is 400
    Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
     result is used in NMS.
post_nms : int, default is 100
    Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
    based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
    number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.wideresnet.IdentityResidualBlock

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.wideresnet.IdentityResidualBlock.__init__
documentation:
Configurable identity-mapping residual block

Parameters
----------
in_channels : int
    Number of input channels.
channels : list of int
    Number of channels in the internal feature maps.
    Can either have two or three elements: if three construct
    a residual block with two `3 x 3` convolutions,
    otherwise construct a bottleneck block with `1 x 1`, then
    `3 x 3` then `1 x 1` convolutions.
stride : int
    Stride of the first `3 x 3` convolution
dilation : int
    Dilation to apply to the `3 x 3` convolutions.
groups : int
    Number of convolution groups.
    This is used to create ResNeXt-style blocks and is only compatible with
    bottleneck blocks.
norm_act : callable
    Function to create normalization / activation Module.
dropout: callable
    Function to create Dropout Module.
dist_bn: Boolean
    A variable to enable or disable use of distributed BN

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.wideresnet.IdentityResidualBlock.hybrid_forward
documentation: This is the standard forward function for non-distributed batch norm
Identity Residual Block for WideResnet

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.wideresnet.WiderResNetA2
documentation:
Wider ResNet with pre-activation (identity mapping) blocks

This variant uses down-sampling by max-pooling in the first two blocks and
 by strided convolution in the others.

Parameters
----------
structure : list of int
    Number of residual blocks in each of the six modules of the network.
norm_act : callable
    Function to create normalization / activation Module.
classes : int
    If not `0` also include global average pooling and a fully-connected layer
    with `classes` outputs at the end
    of the network.
dilation : bool
    If `True` apply dilation to the last three modules and change the
    down-sampling factor from 32 to 8.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.TrackState
documentation:
States of the track.
The track follows the simple state machine as below:

Active: time_since_update always set to 1
    1. If confidence < keep_alive_threshold, goto Missing
    2. If the track is suppressed in track NMS, goto Missing
Missing: every timestep the missing track increment time_since_update by one
    1. If the track is updated again, goto Active
    2. If time_since_update > max_missing, goto Deleted
Deleted: This is an absorbing state

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track.predict
documentation:
Parameters
----------
motion_model : if not None, predict the motion of this track given its history

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track.update
documentation:
Update the state of the track. We override the predicted track position.
Updating the track will keep or flip its state as Active
If the confidence of detection is below the keep_alive_threshold, we will mark this track as missed.
----------
bbx : new detection location of this object
attributes: some useful attributes of this object at this frame, e.g. landmarks

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track.mark_missed
documentation: Mark this track as missed (no association at the current time step).

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track.is_mising
documentation: Returns True if this track is tentative (unconfirmed).

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track.is_active
documentation: Returns True if this track is confirmed.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.utils.Track.is_deleted
documentation: Returns True if this track is dead and should be deleted.

This class represents a track/tracklet used in the SMOT Tracker
It has the following properties

*******************************************************
mean: 4-tuple representing the (x0, y0, x1, y1) as the current state (location) of the tracked object
track_id: the numerical id of the track
age: the number of timesteps since its first occurrence
time_since_update: number of time-steps since the last update of the its location
state: the state of the track, can be one in `TrackState`
confidence_score: tracking_confidence at the current timestep

source: a tuple of (anchor_indices, anchor_weights)
attributes: np.ndarray of additional attributes of the object
*******************************************************

It also has these configs
keep_alive_thresh: the minimal tracking/detection confidence to keep the track in Active state
max_missing: the maximal timesteps we will keep searching for this track when missing before we mark it as deleted
*******************************************************

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.ssd.get_ssd
documentation:
Get SSD models.

# Parameters

name

: Model name, if `None` is used, you must specify `features` to be a `HybridBlock`.

base_size

: Base image size for training, this is fixed once training is assigned.
  A fixed base size still allows you to have variable input size during test.

features

: List of network internal output names, in order to specify which layers are
  used for predicting bbox values.
  If `name` is `None`, `features` must be a `HybridBlock` which generate multiple
  outputs for prediction.

filters

: List of convolution layer channels which is going to be appended to the base
  network feature extractor. If `name` is `None`, this is ignored.

sizes

: Sizes of anchor boxes, this should be a list of floats, in incremental order.
  The length of `sizes` must be len(layers) + 1. For example, a two stage SSD
  model can have `sizes = [30, 60, 90]`, and it converts to `[30, 60]` and
  `[60, 90]` for the two stages, respectively. For more details, please refer
  to original paper.

ratios

: Aspect ratios of anchors in each output layer. Its length must be equals
  to the number of SSD output layers.

steps

: Step size of anchor boxes in each output layer.

classes

: Names of categories.

dataset

: Name of dataset. This is used to identify model name because models trained on
  different datasets are going to be very different.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

ctx

: Context such as mx.cpu(), mx.gpu(0).

root

: Model weights storing path.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A SSD detection network.

documentation:
Set non-maximum suppression parameters.

Parameters

nms_thresh : float, default is 0.45.
    Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.
nms_topk : int, default is 400
    Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
     result is used in NMS.
post_nms : int, default is 100
    Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
    based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
    number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

Returns
-------
None

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.ssd.SSD.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.

# Parameters

classes

: The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.

reuse_weights

: A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
  or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
  This allows the new predictor to reuse the
  previously trained weights specified.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = gluoncv.model_zoo.get_model('ssd_512_resnet50_v1_voc', pretrained=True)
>>> # use direct name to name mapping to reuse weights
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':'person'})
>>> # or use interger mapping, person is the 14th category in VOC
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={0:14})
>>> # you can even mix them
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':14})
>>> # or use a list of string if class name don't change
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights=['person'])
```

Single-shot Object Detection Network: <https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.02325>.

# Parameters

network

: Name of the base network, if `None` is used, will instantiate the
  base network from `features` directly instead of composing.

base_size

: Base input size, it is speficied so SSD can support dynamic input shapes.

features

: Intermediate features to be extracted or a network with multi-output.
  If `network` is `None`, `features` is expected to be a multi-output network.

num_filters

: Number of channels for the appended layers, ignored if `` network`is `None ``.

sizes

: Sizes of anchor boxes, this should be a list of floats, in incremental order.
  The length of `sizes` must be len(layers) + 1. For example, a two stage SSD
  model can have `sizes = [30, 60, 90]`, and it converts to `[30, 60]` and
  `[60, 90]` for the two stages, respectively. For more details, please refer
  to original paper.

ratios

: Aspect ratios of anchors in each output layer. Its length must be equals
  to the number of SSD output layers.

steps

: Step size of anchor boxes in each output layer.

classes

: Names of all categories.

use_1x1_transition

: Whether to use 1x1 convolution as transition layer between attached layers,
  it is effective reducing model capacity.

use_bn

: Whether to use BatchNorm layer after each attached convolutional layer.

reduce_ratio

: Channel reduce ratio (0, 1) of the transition layer.

min_depth

: Minimum channels for the transition layers.

global_pool

: Whether to attach a global average pooling layer as the last output layer.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

stds

: Std values to be divided/multiplied to box encoded values.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

post_nms

: Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
  based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
  number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

anchor_alloc_size

: For advanced users. Define `anchor_alloc_size` to generate large enough anchor
  maps, which will later saved in parameters. During inference, we support arbitrary
  input image by cropping corresponding area of the anchor map. This allow us
  to export to symbol so we can run it in c++, scalar, etc.

ctx

: Network context.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.
  This will only apply to base networks that has `norm_layer` specified, will ignore if the
  base network (e.g. VGG) don't accept this argument.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

root

: The root path for model storage, default is '~/.mxnet/models'

minimal_opset

: We sometimes add special operators to accelerate training/inference, however, for exporting
  to third party compilers we want to utilize most widely used operators.
  If `minimal_opset` is `True`, the network will use a minimal set of operators good
  for e.g., `TVM`.

predictor_kernel: tuple of int. default is (3,3)

: Dimension of predictor kernel

predictor_pad: tuple of int. default is (1,1)

: Padding of the predictor kenrel conv.

anchor_generator: default is SSDAnchorGenerator

: Anchor Generator to be used. The default it SSDAnchorGenerator corresponding
  to SSD published article. This argument can be used for other custom
  anchor generators. Like LiteAnchorGenerator.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.smot_tracker.SMOTTracker.motion_predict
documentation:
Perform motion prediction and assign the predicted track locations to corresponding anchors for re-detection.
It will update the following properties:
    next_frame_pred_index: indices of anchors that bear tracking information. Each track will be assigned to
        several anchors. They will vote in the re-detection processs.
    next_frame_pred_weights: weights in the re-detection voting
    next_frame_pred_bbox: motion-predicted locations of the tracked objects
    waiting_update_tracks: active tracks for re-detection
    next_frame_ref_bbox: original locations of the tracked objects

Parameters
----------
new_frame: BGR frame of this timestep
motion_pred_data: extra data needed by the motion predictor
Returns:
    next_frame_pred_index
    next_frame_pred_weights
-------

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.smot_tracker.SMOTTracker.update

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.smot_tracker.SMOTTracker._motion_prediction
documentation:
Perform motion estimation of a set bounding boxes.
Use either optical flow or SOT algorithms to predict the locations of these bounding boxes in the new frame
Parameters
----------
new_frame
tracked_boxes
tracked_boxes_anchor_indices

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.smot_tracker.SMOTTracker._assign_box_to_anchors
documentation:
The actual implementation of the assignment step.
GPU acceleration is used because the number of anchors is huge
Parameters
----------
boxes: must have >1 boxes
anchors

Returns
-------

Implementation of the SMOT tracker
The steps to use the tracker is:
0. Set anchors from the SSD
1. First call tracker.predict(new_frame)
2. Then get the tracking anchor information
3. Run the detractor with the tracking anchor information
4. Run tracker.update(new_detection, track_info).

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.smot.presets.ssd_300_vgg16_atrous_custom
documentation:
SSD architecture with VGG16 atrous 300x300 base network for COCO.

# Parameters

classes

: Names of custom foreground classes. `len(classes)` is the number of foreground classes.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized.

transfer

: If not `None`, will try to reuse pre-trained weights from SSD networks trained on other
  datasets.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A SSD detection network.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = ssd_300_vgg16_atrous_custom(classes=['a', 'b', 'c'], pretrained_base=True)
>>> net = ssd_300_vgg16_atrous_custom(classes=['foo', 'bar'], transfer='coco')
```

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.vgg.get_vgg
documentation:
VGG model from the `"Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition"
<https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.1556>`_ paper.

Parameters
----------
num_layers : int
    Number of layers for the variant of densenet. Options are 11, 13, 16, 19.
pretrained : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
ctx : Context, default CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default $MXNET_HOME/models
    Location for keeping the model parameters.
Constructors

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.vgg.VGG
documentation:

<https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.1556>`_ paper.

Parameters
----------
layers : list of int
    Numbers of layers in each feature block.
filters : list of int
    Numbers of filters in each feature block. List length should match the layers.
classes : int, default 1000
    Number of classification classes.
batch_norm : bool, default False
    Use batch normalization.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.deeplabv3_plus.get_deeplab_plus
documentation:
DeepLabV3Plus
Parameters
\----------
dataset : str, default pascal_voc

> The dataset that model pretrained on. (pascal_voc, ade20k)

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = get_fcn(dataset='pascal_voc', backbone='xception', pretrained=False)
>>> print(model)
```

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = get_deeplab_plus_xception_coco(pretrained=True)
>>> print(model)
```

DeepLabV3Plus

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of categories for the training dataset.

backbone

: Pre-trained dilated backbone network type (default:'xception').

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used in backbone network (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`;
  for Synchronized Cross-GPU BachNormalization).

aux

: Auxiliary loss.

Reference:

> Chen, Liang-Chieh, et al. "Encoder-Decoder with Atrous Separable Convolution for Semantic
> Image Segmentation."

: Network scale p, t, r.

: Network scale m.Network scale is defined as 36m + 20.

: Number of classification classes.

modules.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.monodepth2_posenet
documentation:
Monodepth
Digging Into Self-Supervised Monocular Depth Estimation, ICCV 2019
https://arxiv.org/abs/1806.01260

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.monodepth2_posenet.get_monodepth2posenet
documentation:
Monodepth2

Parameters
----------
backbone : string
    Pre-trained dilated backbone network type ('resnet18', 'resnet34', 'resnet50',
    'resnet101' or 'resnet152').
pretrained_base : bool or str
    Refers to if the backbone is pretrained or not. If `True`,
    model weights of a model that was trained on ImageNet is loaded.
num_input_images : int
    The number of input sequences. 1 for depth encoder, larger than 1 for pose encoder.
    (Default: 2)
num_input_features : int
    The number of input feature maps from posenet encoder. (Default: 1)
num_frames_to_predict_for: int
    The number of output pose between frames; If None, it equals num_input_features - 1.
    (Default: 2)
stride: int
    The stride number for Conv in pose decoder. (Default: 1)

ctx : Context, default: CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default: '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.
pretrained : bool or str, default: False
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
pretrained_model : string, default: kitti_stereo_640x192
    The dataset that model pretrained on.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.monodepth2_posenet.MonoDepth2PoseNet.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=unused-argument

Monodepth2

# Parameters

backbone

: Pre-trained dilated backbone network type ('resnet18', 'resnet34', 'resnet50',
  'resnet101' or 'resnet152').

pretrained_base

: Refers to if the backbone is pretrained or not. If `True`,
  model weights of a model that was trained on ImageNet is loaded.

num_input_images

: The number of input sequences. 1 for depth encoder, larger than 1 for pose encoder.
  (Default: 2)

num_input_features

: The number of input feature maps from posenet encoder. (Default: 1)

num_frames_to_predict_for: int

: The number of output pose between frames; If None, it equals num_input_features - 1.
  (Default: 2)

stride: int

: The stride number for Conv in pose decoder. (Default: 1)

Reference:

> Clement Godard, Oisin Mac Aodha, Michael Firman, Gabriel Brostow.
> "Digging Into Self-Supervised Monocular Depth Estimation." ICCV, 2019

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = MonoDepth2PoseNet(backbone='resnet18', pretrained_base=True)
>>> print(model)
```

modules.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers

The functions for monodepth2 model and loss

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers.disp_to_depth
documentation:
Convert network's sigmoid output into depth prediction
The formula for this conversion is given in the 'additional considerations'
section of the paper.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers.transformation_from_parameters
documentation: Convert the network's (axisangle, translation) output into a 4x4 matrix

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers.get_translation_matrix
documentation: Convert a translation vector into a 4x4 transformation matrix

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers.rot_from_axisangle
documentation:
Convert an axisangle rotation into a 4x4 transformation matrix
(adapted from https://github.com/Wallacoloo/printipi)
Input 'vec' has to be Bx1x3

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers.get_smooth_loss
documentation:
Computes the smoothness loss for a disparity image
The color image is used for edge-aware smoothness

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.layers.compute_depth_errors
documentation: Computation of error metrics between predicted and ground truth depths

documentation:
MonoDepth2

Parameters

backbone : string, default:'resnet18'
    Pre-trained dilated backbone network type
    ('resnet18', 'resnet34', 'resnet50', 'resnet101' or 'resnet152').

    This will load pretrained backbone network, that was trained on ImageNet.

scales: list, default: range(4)
    The scales used in the loss.
