    The number of output channels.
use_skips: bool, default: True
    This will use skip architecture in the network.

ctx : Context, default: CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default: '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.
pretrained : bool or str, default: False

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.monodepth2.MonoDepth2

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.monodepthv2.monodepth2.MonoDepth2.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=unused-argument

Monodepth2

# Parameters

backbone

: Pre-trained dilated backbone network type ('resnet18', 'resnet34', 'resnet50',
  'resnet101' or 'resnet152').

pretrained_base

: Refers to if the backbone is pretrained or not. If `True`,
  model weights of a model that was trained on ImageNet is loaded.

num_input_images

: The number of input sequences. 1 for depth encoder, larger than 1 for pose encoder.
  (Default: 1)

scales: list

: The scales used in the loss. (Default: range(4))

num_output_channels: int

: The number of output channels. (Default: 1)

use_skips: bool

: This will use skip architecture in the network. (Default: True)

Reference:

> Clement Godard, Oisin Mac Aodha, Michael Firman, Gabriel Brostow.
> "Digging Into Self-Supervised Monocular Depth Estimation." ICCV, 2019

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = MonoDepth2(backbone='resnet18', pretrained_base=True)
>>> print(model)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo_target.YOLOV3PrefetchTargetGenerator

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo_target.YOLOV3PrefetchTargetGenerator.forward
documentation:
Generating training targets that do not require network predictions.

Parameters
----------
img : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Original image tensor.
xs : list of mxnet.nd.NDArray
    List of feature maps.
anchors : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    YOLO3 anchors.
offsets : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Pre-generated x and y offsets for YOLO3.
gt_boxes : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Ground-truth boxes.
gt_ids : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Ground-truth IDs.
gt_mixratio : mxnet.nd.NDArray, optional
    Mixup ratio from 0 to 1.

Returns
-------
(tuple of) mxnet.nd.NDArray
    objectness: 0 for negative, 1 for positive, -1 for ignore.
    center_targets: regression target for center x and y.
    scale_targets: regression target for scale x and y.
    weights: element-wise gradient weights for center_targets and scale_targets.
    class_targets: a one-hot vector for classification.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo_target.YOLOV3PrefetchTargetGenerator._slice
documentation: since some stages won't see partial anchors, so we have to slice the correct targets

YOLO V3 prefetch target generator.
The target generated by this instance is invariant to network predictions.
Therefore it is usually used in DataLoader transform function to reduce the load on GPUs.

Parameters
----------
num_class : int
    Number of foreground classes.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo_target.YOLOV3DynamicTargetGeneratorSimple

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo_target.YOLOV3DynamicTargetGeneratorSimple.hybrid_forward
documentation:
Short summary.

Parameters
----------
F : mxnet.nd or mxnet.sym
    `F` is mxnet.sym if hybridized or mxnet.nd if not.
box_preds : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Predicted bounding boxes.
gt_boxes : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Ground-truth bounding boxes.

Returns
-------
(tuple of) mxnet.nd.NDArray
    objectness: 0 for negative, 1 for positive, -1 for ignore.
    center_targets: regression target for center x and y.
    scale_targets: regression target for scale x and y.
    weights: element-wise gradient weights for center_targets and scale_targets.
    class_targets: a one-hot vector for classification.

YOLOV3 target generator that requires network predictions.
`Dynamic` indicate that the targets generated depend on current network.
`Simple` indicate that it only support `pos_iou_thresh` >= 1.0,
otherwise it's a lot more complicated and slower.
(box regression targets and class targets are not necessary when `pos_iou_thresh` >= 1.0)

Parameters
----------
num_class : int
    Number of foreground classes.
ignore_iou_thresh : float
    Anchors that has IOU in `range(ignore_iou_thresh, pos_iou_thresh)` don't get
    penalized of objectness score.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.darknet.get_darknet
documentation:
Get darknet by `version` and `num_layers` info.

# Parameters

darknet_version

: Darknet version, choices are ['v3'].

num_layers

: Number of layers.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

# Returns

mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock

: Darknet network.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = get_darknet('v3', 53, pretrained=True)
>>> print(model)
```

modules.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn
documentation: RCNN Model.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn.custom_rcnn_fpn
documentation:
Generate custom RCNN model with resnet base network w/FPN.

Parameters
----------
pretrained_base : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
base_network_name : str, default 'resnet18_v1b'
    base network for mask RCNN. Currently support: 'resnet18_v1b', 'resnet50_v1b',
    and 'resnet101_v1d'
norm_layer : nn.HybridBlock, default nn.BatchNorm
    Gluon normalization layer to use. Default is frozen batch normalization layer.
norm_kwargs : dict
    Keyword arguments for gluon normalization layer
sym_norm_layer : nn.SymbolBlock, default `None`
    Symbol normalization layer to use in FPN. This is due to FPN being implemented using
    SymbolBlock. Default is `None`, meaning no normalization layer will be used in FPN.
sym_norm_kwargs : dict
    Keyword arguments for symbol normalization layer used in FPN.
num_fpn_filters : int, default 256
    Number of filters for FPN output layers.
num_box_head_conv : int, default 4
    Number of convolution layers to use in box head if batch normalization is not frozen.
num_box_head_conv_filters : int, default 256
    Number of filters for convolution layers in box head.
    Only applicable if batch normalization is not frozen.
num_box_head_dense_filters : int, default 1024
    Number of hidden units for the last fully connected layer in box head.

Returns
-------
SymbolBlock or HybridBlock
    Base feature extractor eg. resnet w/ FPN.
None or HybridBlock
    R-CNN feature before each task heads.
HybridBlock
    Box feature extractor

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn.RCNN

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn.RCNN.collect_train_params
documentation:
Collect trainable params.

This function serves as a help utility function to return only
trainable parameters if predefined by experienced developer/researcher.
For example, if cross-device BatchNorm is not enabled, we will definitely
want to fix BatchNorm statistics to avoid scaling problem because RCNN training
batch size is usually very small.

# Parameters

select

: Regular expressions for parameter match pattern

# Returns

The selected {py:class}`mxnet.gluon.ParameterDict`

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn.RCNN.set_nms
documentation:
Set NMS parameters to the network.

:::{Note}
If you are using hybrid mode, make sure you re-hybridize after calling
`set_nms`.
:::

# Parameters

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

force_nms

: Appy NMS to all categories, this is to avoid overlapping detection results
  from different categories.

# Returns

None

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn.RCNN.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.

Parameters
----------
classes : iterable of str
    The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.
reuse_weights : dict
    A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
    or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
    This allows the new predictor to reuse the
    previously trained weights specified.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rcnn.RCNN.hybrid_forward
documentation: Not implemented yet.
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

RCNN network.

# Parameters

features

: Base feature extractor before feature pooling layer.

top_features

: Tail feature extractor after feature pooling layer.

classes

: Names of categories, its length is `num_class`.

box_features

: feature head for transforming roi output for box prediction.

short

: Input image short side size.

max_size

: Maximum size of input image long side.

train_patterns

: Matching pattern for trainable parameters.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

roi_mode

: ROI pooling mode. Currently support 'pool' and 'align'.

roi_size

: (height, width) of the ROI region.

strides

: Stride(s) of network features. Tuple for FPN.

clip: float

: Clip bounding box target to this value.

# Attributes

classes

: Names of categories, its length is `num_class`.

num_class

: Number of positive categories.

short

: Input image short side size.

max_size

: Maximum size of input image long side.

train_patterns

: Matching pattern for trainable parameters.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

minimal_opset

: We sometimes add special operators to accelerate training/inference, however, for exporting
  to third party compilers we want to utilize most widely used operators.
  If `minimal_opset` is `True`, the network will use a minimal set of operators good
  for e.g., `TVM`.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo3.get_yolov3
documentation:
Get YOLOV3 models.
Parameters
\----------
name : str or None

> Model name, if `None` is used, you must specify `features` to be a `HybridBlock`.

stages

: List of network internal output names, in order to specify which layers are
  used for predicting bbox values.
  If `name` is `None`, `features` must be a `HybridBlock` which generate multiple
  outputs for prediction.

filters

: List of convolution layer channels which is going to be appended to the base
  network feature extractor. If `name` is `None`, this is ignored.

sizes

: Sizes of anchor boxes, this should be a list of floats, in incremental order.
  The length of `sizes` must be len(layers) + 1. For example, a two stage SSD
  model can have `sizes = [30, 60, 90]`, and it converts to `[30, 60]` and
  `[60, 90]` for the two stages, respectively. For more details, please refer
  to original paper.

ratios

: Aspect ratios of anchors in each output layer. Its length must be equals
  to the number of SSD output layers.

steps

: Step size of anchor boxes in each output layer.

classes

: Names of categories.

dataset

: Name of dataset. This is used to identify model name because models trained on
  different datasets are going to be very different.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

ctx

: Context such as mx.cpu(), mx.gpu(0).

root

: Model weights storing path.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

# Returns

HybridBlock

: A YOLOV3 detection network.

Hybrid Forward of YOLOV3Output layer.

    `F` is mxnet.sym if hybridized or mxnet.nd if not.

Returns

    During training, return (bbox, raw_box_centers, raw_box_scales, objness,
    class_pred, anchors, offsets).
    During inference, return detections.

YOLO output layer V3.
Parameters
----------
index : int
    Index of the yolo output layer, to avoid naming conflicts only.
num_class : int
    Number of foreground objects.
anchors : iterable
    The anchor setting. Reference: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1804.02767.pdf.
stride : int
    Stride of feature map.
alloc_size : tuple of int, default is (128, 128)
    For advanced users. Define `alloc_size` to generate large enough anchor
    maps, which will later saved in parameters. During inference, we support arbitrary
    input image by cropping corresponding area of the anchor map. This allow us
    to export to symbol so we can run it in c++, Scalar, etc.

documentation:
YOLOV3 network hybrid forward.
Parameters

F : mxnet.nd or mxnet.sym
    `F` is mxnet.sym if hybridized or mxnet.nd if not.
x : mxnet.nd.NDArray
    Input data.
*args : optional, mxnet.nd.NDArray

    (gt_boxes, obj_t, centers_t, scales_t, weights_t, clas_t)

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo3.YOLOV3.set_nms
documentation:
Set non-maximum suppression parameters.
Parameters
----------
nms_thresh : float, default is 0.45.
    Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.
nms_topk : int, default is 400
    Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
     result is used in NMS.
post_nms : int, default is 100
    Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
    based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
    number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.
Returns
-------
None

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.yolo.yolo3.YOLOV3.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.
Parameters
\----------
classes : iterable of str

> The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.

reuse_weights

: A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
  or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
  This allows the new predictor to reuse the
  previously trained weights specified.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = gluoncv.model_zoo.get_model('yolo3_darknet53_voc', pretrained=True)
>>> # use direct name to name mapping to reuse weights
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':'person'})
>>> # or use interger mapping, person is the 14th category in VOC
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={0:14})
>>> # you can even mix them
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':14})
>>> # or use a list of string if class name don't change
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights=['person'])
```

YOLO V3 detection network.
Reference: <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1804.02767.pdf>.
Parameters
\----------
stages : mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock

> Staged feature extraction blocks.
> For example, 3 stages and 3 YOLO output layers are used original paper.

channels

: Number of conv channels for each appended stage.
  `len(channels)` should match `len(stages)`.

num_class

: Number of foreground objects.

anchors

: The anchor setting. `len(anchors)` should match `len(stages)`.

strides

: Strides of feature map. `len(strides)` should match `len(stages)`.

alloc_size

: For advanced users. Define `alloc_size` to generate large enough anchor
  maps, which will later saved in parameters. During inference, we support arbitrary
  input image by cropping corresponding area of the anchor map. This allow us
  to export to symbol so we can run it in c++, Scalar, etc.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

post_nms

: Only return top `post_nms` detection results, the rest is discarded. The number is
  based on COCO dataset which has maximum 100 objects per image. You can adjust this
  number if expecting more objects. You can use -1 to return all detections.

pos_iou_thresh

: IOU threshold for true anchors that match real objects.
  'pos_iou_thresh < 1' is not implemented.

ignore_iou_thresh

: Anchors that has IOU in `range(ignore_iou_thresh, pos_iou_thresh)` don't get
  penalized of objectness score.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rpn.proposal.RPNProposal

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rpn.proposal.RPNProposal.hybrid_forward
documentation: Generate proposals.
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

Proposal generator for RPN.

RPNProposal takes RPN anchors, RPN prediction scores and box regression predictions.
It will transform anchors, apply NMS (if set to true) to get clean foreground proposals.

# Parameters

clip

: Clip bounding box target to this value.

min_size

: Proposals whose size is smaller than `min_size` will be discarded.

stds

: Standard deviation to be multiplied from encoded regression targets.
  These values must be the same as stds used in RPNTargetGenerator.

nms

: Whether to do nms.

minimal_opset

: We sometimes add special operators to accelerate training/inference, however, for exporting
  to third party compilers we want to utilize most widely used operators.
  If `minimal_opset` is `True`, the network will use a minimal set of operators good
  for e.g., `TVM`.

Returns

mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock
    The Mask RCNN network.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.mask_rcnn.mask_rcnn.custom_mask_rcnn_fpn
documentation:
Mask RCNN model with resnet base network and FPN on custom dataset.

Parameters
----------
classes : iterable of str
    Names of custom foreground classes. `len(classes)` is the number of foreground classes.
transfer : str or None
    Dataset from witch to transfer from. If not `None`, will try to reuse pre-trained weights
    from faster RCNN networks trained on other dataset, specified by the parameter.
dataset : str, default 'custom'
    Dataset name attached to the network name
pretrained_base : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
base_network_name : str, default 'resnet18_v1b'
    base network for mask RCNN. Currently support: 'resnet18_v1b', 'resnet50_v1b',
    and 'resnet101_v1d'
norm_layer : nn.HybridBlock, default nn.BatchNorm
    Gluon normalization layer to use. Default is frozen batch normalization layer.
norm_kwargs : dict
    Keyword arguments for gluon normalization layer
sym_norm_layer : nn.SymbolBlock, default `None`
    Symbol normalization layer to use in FPN. This is due to FPN being implemented using
    SymbolBlock. Default is `None`, meaning no normalization layer will be used in FPN.
sym_norm_kwargs : dict
    Keyword arguments for symbol normalization layer used in FPN.
num_fpn_filters : int, default 256
    Number of filters for FPN output layers.
num_box_head_conv : int, default 4
    Number of convolution layers to use in box head if batch normalization is not frozen.
num_box_head_conv_filters : int, default 256
    Number of filters for convolution layers in box head.
    Only applicable if batch normalization is not frozen.
num_box_head_dense_filters : int, default 1024
    Number of hidden units for the last fully connected layer in box head.
ctx : Context, default CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.

Returns
-------
mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock
    Hybrid faster RCNN network.

    boxes, segmentation masks.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.mask_rcnn.mask_rcnn.MaskRCNN.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.

# Parameters

classes

: The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.

reuse_weights

: A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
  or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
  This allows the new predictor to reuse the
  previously trained weights specified.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = gluoncv.model_zoo.get_model('mask_rcnn_resnet50_v1b_voc', pretrained=True)
>>> # use direct name to name mapping to reuse weights
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':'person'})
>>> # or use interger mapping, person is the first category in COCO
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={0:0})
>>> # you can even mix them
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':0})
>>> # or use a list of string if class name don't change
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights=['person'])
```

Mask RCNN network.

# Parameters

features

: Base feature extractor before feature pooling layer.

top_features

: Tail feature extractor after feature pooling layer.

classes

: Names of categories, its length is `num_class`.

mask_channels

: Number of channels in mask prediction

rcnn_max_dets

: Number of rois to retain in RCNN.
  Upper bounded by min of rpn_test_pre_nms and rpn_test_post_nms.

rpn_test_pre_nms

: Filter top proposals before NMS in testing of RPN.

rpn_test_post_nms

: Return top proposal results after NMS in testing of RPN.
  Will be set to rpn_test_pre_nms if it is larger than rpn_test_pre_nms.

target_roi_scale

: Ratio of mask output roi / input roi. For model with FPN, this is typically 2.

num_fcn_convs

: number of convolution blocks before deconv layer. For FPN network this is typically 4.

documentation:
ResNet V1 model from ["Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition"](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385) paper.
ResNet V2 model from ["Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1603.05027) paper.

# Parameters

version

: Version of ResNet. Options are 1, 2.

num_layers

: Numbers of layers. Options are 18, 34, 50, 101, 152.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

use_se

: Whether to use Squeeze-and-Excitation module

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

documentation:
BasicBlock V1 from ["Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition"](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385) paper.
This is used for ResNet V1 for 18, 34 layers.

# Parameters

channels

: Number of output channels.

stride

: Stride size.

downsample

: Whether to downsample the input.

in_channels

: Number of input channels. Default is 0, to infer from the graph.

last_gamma

: Whether to initialize the gamma of the last BatchNorm layer in each bottleneck to zero.

use_se

: Whether to use Squeeze-and-Excitation module

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

Slice anchors given the input image shape.

Outputs:

pylint: disable=arguments-differ

Anchor generator for Region Proposal Networks.

# Parameters

stride

: Feature map stride with respect to original image.
  This is usually the ratio between original image size and feature map size.

base_size

: The width(and height) of reference anchor box.

ratios

: The aspect ratios of anchor boxes. We expect it to be a list or tuple.

scales

: The areas of anchor boxes.
  We use the following form to compute the shapes of anchors:

  $$
  width_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ 1 / ratio}
  height_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ratio}
  $$

alloc_size

: Allocate size for the anchor boxes as (H, W).
  Usually we generate enough anchors for large feature map, e.g. 128x128.
  Later in inference we can have variable input sizes,
  at which time we can crop corresponding anchors from this large
  anchor map so we can skip re-generating anchors for each input.

A sampler to choose positive/negative samples from RPN anchors

# Parameters

num_sample

: Number of samples for RPN targets.

pos_iou_thresh

: Proposal whose IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.

neg_iou_thresh

: Proposal whose IOU smaller than `neg_iou_thresh` is regarded as negative samples.

pos_ratio

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is
  to be sampled.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rpn.rpn_target.RPNTargetGenerator.forward
documentation:
RPNTargetGenerator is only used in data transform with no batch dimension.
Be careful there's numpy operations inside

Parameters
----------
bbox: (M, 4) ground truth boxes with corner encoding.
anchor: (N, 4) anchor boxes with corner encoding.
width: int width of input image
height: int height of input image

Returns
-------
cls_target: (N,) value +1: pos, 0: neg, -1: ignore
box_target: (N, 4) only anchors whose cls_target > 0 has nonzero box target
box_mask: (N, 4) only anchors whose cls_target > 0 has nonzero mask
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

RPN target generator network.

# Parameters

num_sample

: Number of samples for RPN targets.

pos_iou_thresh

: Anchor with IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.

neg_iou_thresh

: Anchor with IOU smaller than `neg_iou_thresh` is regarded as negative samples.
  Anchors with IOU in between `pos_iou_thresh` and `neg_iou_thresh` are
  ignored.

pos_ratio

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is
  to be sampled.

stds

: Std value to be divided from encoded regression targets.

allowed_border

: The allowed distance of anchors which are off the image border. This is used to clip out of
  border anchors. You can set it to very large value to keep all anchors.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.mask_rcnn.predefined_models.mask_rcnn_resnet50_v1b_coco
documentation:
Mask RCNN model from the paper
"He, K., Gkioxari, G., Doll&ar, P., & Girshick, R. (2017). Mask R-CNN"

# Parameters

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

pretrained_base

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = mask_rcnn_resnet50_v1b_coco(pretrained=True)
>>> print(model)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rpn.rpn.RPN

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.rpn.rpn.RPN.hybrid_forward
documentation:
Forward RPN.

The behavior during training and inference is different.

Parameters
----------
img : mxnet.nd.NDArray or mxnet.symbol
    The original input image.
x : mxnet.nd.NDArray or mxnet.symbol(s)
    Feature tensor(s).

Returns
-------
(rpn_score, rpn_box)
    Returns predicted scores and regions which are candidates of objects.
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

Region Proposal Network.

# Parameters

channels

: Channel number used in convolutional layers.

strides

: Feature map stride with respect to original image.
  This is usually the ratio between original image size and feature map size.

base_size

: The width(and height) of reference anchor box.

scales

: The areas of anchor boxes.
  We use the following form to compute the shapes of anchors:

  $$
  width_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ 1 / ratio}
  height_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ratio}
  $$

ratios

: The aspect ratios of anchor boxes. We expect it to be a list or tuple.

alloc_size

: Allocate size for the anchor boxes as (H, W).
  Usually we generate enough anchors for large feature map, e.g. 128x128.
  Later in inference we can have variable input sizes,
  at which time we can crop corresponding anchors from this large
  anchor map so we can skip re-generating anchors for each input.

clip

: Clip bounding box target to this value.

nms_thresh

: IOU threshold for NMS. It is used to remove overlapping proposals.

train_pre_nms

: Filter top proposals before NMS in training.

train_post_nms

: Return top proposal results after NMS in training.

test_pre_nms

: Filter top proposals before NMS in testing.

test_post_nms

: Return top proposal results after NMS in testing.

min_size

: Proposals whose size is smaller than `min_size` will be discarded.
  multi_level : boolean
  Whether to extract feature from multiple level. This is used in FPN.

multi_level

: Whether to use multiple feature maps for RPN. eg. FPN.

per_level_nms

: Whether to apply nms on each level's rois instead of applying nms after aggregation.

minimal_opset

: We sometimes add special operators to accelerate training/inference, however, for exporting
  to third party compilers we want to utilize most widely used operators.
  If `minimal_opset` is `True`, the network will use a minimal set of operators good
  for e.g., `TVM`.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.rcnn_target.RCNNTargetSampler.hybrid_forward
documentation:
Handle B=self._num_image by a for loop.

Parameters
----------
rois: (B, self._num_proposal, 4) encoded in (x1, y1, x2, y2).
scores: (B, self._num_proposal, 1), value range [0, 1] with ignore value -1.
gt_boxes: (B, M, 4) encoded in (x1, y1, x2, y2), invalid box should have area of 0.

Returns
-------
rois: (B, self._num_sample, 4), randomly drawn from proposals
samples: (B, self._num_sample), value +1: positive / 0: ignore / -1: negative.
matches: (B, self._num_sample), value between [0, M)
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

A sampler to choose positive/negative samples from RCNN Proposals

# Parameters

num_image: int

: Number of input images.

num_proposal: int

: Number of input proposals.

num_sample

: Number of samples for RCNN targets.

pos_iou_thresh

: Proposal whose IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.
  Proposal whose IOU smaller than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as negative samples.

pos_ratio

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is
  to be sampled.

max_num_gt

: Maximum ground-truth number for each example. This is only an upper bound, not
  necessarily very precise. However, using a very big number may impact the training speed.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.rcnn_target.RCNNTargetGenerator

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.rcnn_target.RCNNTargetGenerator.hybrid_forward
documentation:
Components can handle batch images

Parameters
----------
roi: (B, N, 4), input proposals
samples: (B, N), value +1: positive / -1: negative.
matches: (B, N), value [0, M), index to gt_label and gt_box.
gt_label: (B, M), value [0, num_class), excluding background class.
gt_box: (B, M, 4), input ground truth box corner coordinates.

Returns
-------
cls_target: (B, N), value [0, num_class + 1), including background.
box_target: (B, N, C, 4), only foreground class has nonzero target.
box_weight: (B, N, C, 4), only foreground class has nonzero weight.
pylint: disable=arguments-differ, unused-argument

RCNN target encoder to generate matching target and regression target values.

Parameters
----------
num_class : int
    Number of total number of positive classes.
max_pos : int, default is 128
    Upper bound of Number of positive samples.
per_device_batch_size : int, default is 1
    Per device batch size
means : iterable of float, default is (0., 0., 0., 0.)
    Mean values to be subtracted from regression targets.
stds : iterable of float, default is (.1, .1, .2, .2)
    Standard deviations to be divided from regression targets.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.doublehead_rcnn.get_doublehead_rcnn
documentation:
Utility function to return faster rcnn networks.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    Model name.
dataset : str
    The name of dataset.
pretrained : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
ctx : mxnet.Context
    Context such as mx.cpu(), mx.gpu(0).
root : str
    Model weights storing path.

Returns
-------
mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock
    The DoubleHeadRCNN-RCNN network.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.doublehead_rcnn.custom_faster_rcnn_fpn
documentation:
Faster RCNN model with resnet base network and FPN on custom dataset.

Parameters
----------
classes : iterable of str
    Names of custom foreground classes. `len(classes)` is the number of foreground classes.
transfer : str or None
    Dataset from witch to transfer from. If not `None`, will try to reuse pre-trained weights
    from faster RCNN networks trained on other dataset, specified by the parameter.
dataset : str, default 'custom'
    Dataset name attached to the network name
pretrained_base : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
base_network_name : str, default 'resnet18_v1b'
    base network for mask RCNN. Currently support: 'resnet18_v1b', 'resnet50_v1b',
    and 'resnet101_v1d'
norm_layer : nn.HybridBlock, default nn.BatchNorm
    Gluon normalization layer to use. Default is frozen batch normalization layer.
norm_kwargs : dict
    Keyword arguments for gluon normalization layer
sym_norm_layer : nn.SymbolBlock, default `None`
    Symbol normalization layer to use in FPN. This is due to FPN being implemented using
    SymbolBlock. Default is `None`, meaning no normalization layer will be used in FPN.
sym_norm_kwargs : dict
    Keyword arguments for symbol normalization layer used in FPN.
num_fpn_filters : int, default 256
    Number of filters for FPN output layers.
num_box_head_conv : int, default 4
    Number of convolution layers to use in box head if batch normalization is not frozen.
num_box_head_conv_filters : int, default 256
    Number of filters for convolution layers in box head.
    Only applicable if batch normalization is not frozen.
num_box_head_dense_filters : int, default 1024
    Number of hidden units for the last fully connected layer in box head.
ctx : Context, default CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.

Returns
-------
mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock
    Hybrid faster RCNN network.

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.doublehead_rcnn.BottleneckV1
documentation:
Bottleneck V1 from `"Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition"
<http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385>`_ paper.
This is used for ResNet V1 for 50, 101, 152 layers.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.doublehead_rcnn.DoubleHeadRCNN.reset_class
documentation:
Reset class categories and class predictors.

# Parameters

classes

: The new categories. ['apple', 'orange'] for example.

reuse_weights

: A {new_integer : old_integer} or mapping dict or {new_name : old_name} mapping dict,
  or a list of [name0, name1,...] if class names don't change.
  This allows the new predictor to reuse the
  previously trained weights specified.

# Example

```pycon
>>> net = gluoncv.model_zoo.get_model('faster_rcnn_resnet50_v1b_coco', pretrained=True)
>>> # use direct name to name mapping to reuse weights
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':'person'})
>>> # or use interger mapping, person is the 14th category in VOC
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={0:14})
>>> # you can even mix them
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights={'person':14})
>>> # or use a list of string if class name don't change
>>> net.reset_class(classes=['person'], reuse_weights=['person'])
```

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.doublehead_rcnn.DoubleHeadRCNN._pyramid_roi_feats
documentation:
Assign rpn_rois to specific FPN layers according to its area
   and then perform `ROIPooling` or `ROIAlign` to generate final
   region proposals aggregated features.
Parameters
----------
features : list of mx.ndarray or mx.symbol
    Features extracted from FPN base network
rpn_rois : mx.ndarray or mx.symbol
    (N, 5) with [[batch_index, x1, y1, x2, y2], ...] like
roi_size : tuple
    The size of each roi with regard to ROI-Wise operation
    each region proposal will be roi_size spatial shape.
strides : tuple e.g. [4, 8, 16, 32]
    Define the gap between each feature in feature map in the original image space.
roi_mode : str, default is align
    ROI pooling mode. Currently support 'pool' and 'align'.
roi_canonical_scale : float, default is 224.0
    Hyperparameters for the RoI-to-FPN level mapping heuristic.
sampling_ratio : int, default is 2
    number of inputs samples to take for each output
    sample. 0 to take samples densely.
Returns
-------
Pooled roi features aggregated according to its roi_level

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.rcnn.faster_rcnn.doublehead_rcnn.DoubleHeadRCNN.hybrid_forward
documentation:
Forward DoubleHeadRCNN-RCNN network.

The behavior during training and inference is different.

Parameters
----------
x : mxnet.nd.NDArray or mxnet.symbol
    The network input tensor.
gt_box : type, only required during training
    The ground-truth bbox tensor with shape (B, N, 4).
gt_label : type, only required during training
    The ground-truth label tensor with shape (B, 1, 4).

Returns
-------
(ids, scores, bboxes)
    During inference, returns final class id, confidence scores, bounding
    boxes.
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

Double Head RCNN network.

# Parameters

features

: Base feature extractor before feature pooling layer.

top_features

: Tail feature extractor after feature pooling layer.

classes

: Names of categories, its length is `num_class`.

box_features

: feature head for transforming shared ROI output (top_features) for box prediction.
  If set to None, global average pooling will be used.

short

: Input image short side size.

max_size

: Maximum size of input image long side.

min_stage

: Minimum stage NO. for FPN stages.

max_stage

: Maximum stage NO. for FPN stages.

train_patterns

: Matching pattern for trainable parameters.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk

: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
  result is used in NMS.

roi_mode

: ROI pooling mode. Currently support 'pool' and 'align'.

roi_size

: (height, width) of the ROI region.

strides

: Feature map stride with respect to original image.
  This is usually the ratio between original image size and feature map size.
  For FPN, use a tuple of ints.

clip

: Clip bounding box prediction to to prevent exponentiation from overflowing.

rpn_channel

: number of channels used in RPN convolutional layers.

base_size

: The width(and height) of reference anchor box.

scales

: The areas of anchor boxes.
  We use the following form to compute the shapes of anchors:

  $$
  width_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ 1 / ratio}
  height_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ratio}
  $$

ratios

: The aspect ratios of anchor boxes. We expect it to be a list or tuple.

alloc_size

: Allocate size for the anchor boxes as (H, W).
  Usually we generate enough anchors for large feature map, e.g. 128x128.
  Later in inference we can have variable input sizes,
  at which time we can crop corresponding anchors from this large
  anchor map so we can skip re-generating anchors for each input.

rpn_train_pre_nms

: Filter top proposals before NMS in training of RPN.

rpn_train_post_nms

: Return top proposal results after NMS in training of RPN.
  Will be set to rpn_train_pre_nms if it is larger than rpn_train_pre_nms.

rpn_test_pre_nms

: Filter top proposals before NMS in testing of RPN.

rpn_test_post_nms

: Return top proposal results after NMS in testing of RPN.
  Will be set to rpn_test_pre_nms if it is larger than rpn_test_pre_nms.

rpn_nms_thresh

: IOU threshold for NMS. It is used to remove overlapping proposals.

rpn_num_sample

: Number of samples for RPN targets.

rpn_pos_iou_thresh

: Anchor with IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.

rpn_neg_iou_thresh

: Anchor with IOU smaller than `neg_iou_thresh` is regarded as negative samples.
  Anchors with IOU in between `pos_iou_thresh` and `neg_iou_thresh` are
  ignored.

rpn_pos_ratio

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is
  to be sampled.

rpn_box_norm

: Std value to be divided from encoded values.

rpn_min_size

: Proposals whose size is smaller than `min_size` will be discarded.

per_device_batch_size

: Batch size for each device during training.

num_sample

: Number of samples for RCNN targets.

pos_iou_thresh

: Proposal whose IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.

pos_ratio

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is
  to be sampled.

max_num_gt

: Maximum ground-truth number for each example. This is only an upper bound, not
  necessarily very precise. However, using a very big number may impact the training speed.

additional_output

: `additional_output` is only used for Mask R-CNN to get internal outputs.

force_nms

: Appy NMS to all categories, this is to avoid overlapping detection results from different
  categories.

minimal_opset

: We sometimes add special operators to accelerate training/inference, however, for exporting
  to third party compilers we want to utilize most widely used operators.
  If `minimal_opset` is `True`, the network will use a minimal set of operators good
  for e.g., `TVM`.

# Attributes

classes

: Names of categories, its length is `num_class`.

num_class

: Number of positive categories.

short

: Input image short side size.

max_size

: Maximum size of input image long side.

train_patterns

: Matching pattern for trainable parameters.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk
: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection

  : result is used in NMS.

force_nms

: Appy NMS to all categories, this is to avoid overlapping detection results
  from different categories.

rpn_target_generator

: Generate training targets with cls_target, box_target, and box_mask.

target_generator

: Generate training targets with boxes, samples, matches, gt_label and gt_box.

Faster RCNN network.

# Parameters

features

: Base feature extractor before feature pooling layer.

: Minimum stage NO. for FPN stages.

max_stage

: Maximum stage NO. for FPN stages.

: Matching pattern for trainable parameters.

nms_thresh

: Non-maximum suppression threshold. You can specify < 0 or > 1 to disable NMS.

nms_topk

: Apply NMS to top k detection results, use -1 to disable so that every Detection
  result is used in NMS.

roi_mode

roi_size

: (height, width) of the ROI region.

  This is usually the ratio between original image size and feature map size.
  For FPN, use a tuple of ints.

clip

: Clip bounding box prediction to to prevent exponentiation from overflowing.

: number of channels used in RPN convolutional layers.

base_size

: The width(and height) of reference anchor box.

scales

: The areas of anchor boxes.
  We use the following form to compute the shapes of anchors:

  $$
  width_{anchor} = size_{base} \times scale \times \sqrt{ 1 / ratio}

: The aspect ratios of anchor boxes. We expect it to be a list or tuple.

: Allocate size for the anchor boxes as (H, W).
  Usually we generate enough anchors for large feature map, e.g. 128x128.
  Later in inference we can have variable input sizes,
  at which time we can crop corresponding anchors from this large
  anchor map so we can skip re-generating anchors for each input.

: Filter top proposals before NMS in training of RPN.

rpn_train_post_nms

: Return top proposal results after NMS in training of RPN.
  Will be set to rpn_train_pre_nms if it is larger than rpn_train_pre_nms.

: Number of samples for RPN targets.

rpn_pos_iou_thresh

: Anchor with IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is

: Std value to be divided from encoded values.

rpn_min_size

: Proposals whose size is smaller than `min_size` will be discarded.

per_device_batch_size

: Batch size for each device during training.

num_sample

: Number of samples for RCNN targets.

pos_iou_thresh

: Proposal whose IOU larger than `pos_iou_thresh` is regarded as positive samples.

pos_ratio

: `pos_ratio` defines how many positive samples (`pos_ratio * num_sample`) is

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.squeezenet.get_squeezenet
documentation:
SqueezeNet model from the `"SqueezeNet: AlexNet-level accuracy with 50x fewer parameters
and <0.5MB model size" <https://arxiv.org/abs/1602.07360>`_ paper.
SqueezeNet 1.1 model from the `official SqueezeNet repo
<https://github.com/DeepScale/SqueezeNet/tree/master/SqueezeNet_v1.1>`_.
SqueezeNet 1.1 has 2.4x less computation and slightly fewer parameters
than SqueezeNet 1.0, without sacrificing accuracy.

Parameters
----------
version : str
    Version of squeezenet. Options are '1.0', '1.1'.
pretrained : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
ctx : Context, default CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default $MXNET_HOME/models
    Location for keeping the model parameters.
Constructor

and <0.5MB model size" <https://arxiv.org/abs/1602.07360>`_ paper.
SqueezeNet 1.1 model from the `official SqueezeNet repo
<https://github.com/DeepScale/SqueezeNet/tree/master/SqueezeNet_v1.1>`_.
SqueezeNet 1.1 has 2.4x less computation and slightly fewer parameters
than SqueezeNet 1.0, without sacrificing accuracy.

Parameters
----------
version : str
    Version of squeezenet. Options are '1.0', '1.1'.
classes : int, default 1000
    Number of classification classes.
Net

modules.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.fcn

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.fcn.get_fcn
documentation:
FCN model from the paper ["Fully Convolutional Network for semantic segmentation"](https://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~jonlong/long_shelhamer_fcn.pdf)

# Parameters

dataset

: The dataset that model pretrained on. (pascal_voc, ade20k)

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

pretrained_base

: This will load pretrained backbone network, that was trained on ImageNet.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = get_fcn(dataset='pascal_voc', backbone='resnet50', pretrained=False)
>>> print(model)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.fcn.FCN

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.fcn.FCN.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=arguments-differ

Fully Convolutional Networks for Semantic Segmentation

# Parameters

nclass

: Number of categories for the training dataset.

backbone

: Pre-trained dilated backbone network type (default:'resnet50'; 'resnet50',
  'resnet101' or 'resnet152').

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used in backbone network (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`;

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

pretrained_base

: Refers to if the FCN backbone or the encoder is pretrained or not. If `True`,
  model weights of a model that was trained on ImageNet is loaded.

Reference:

> Long, Jonathan, Evan Shelhamer, and Trevor Darrell. "Fully convolutional networks
> for semantic segmentation." *CVPR*, 2015

# Examples

```pycon
>>> model = FCN(nclass=21, backbone='resnet50')
>>> print(model)
```

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.model_store.get_model_file
documentation:
Return location for the pretrained on local file system.

This function will download from online model zoo when model cannot be found or has mismatch.
The root directory will be created if it doesn't exist.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    Name of the model.
root : str, default '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.

Returns
-------
file_path
    Path to the requested pretrained model file.

"Ren, S., He, K., Girshick, R., & Sun, J. (2015). Faster r-cnn: Towards
real-time object detection with region proposal networks"

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.

: Load pretrained base network, the extra layers are randomized. Note that
  if pretrained is `True`, this has no effect.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

Parameters
----------
classes : iterable of str
    Names of custom foreground classes. `len(classes)` is the number of foreground classes.
transfer : str or None
    If not `None`, will try to reuse pre-trained weights from faster RCNN networks trained
    on other datasets.
pretrained : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
pretrained_base : bool or str
    Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
    String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.
ctx : Context, default CPU
    The context in which to load the pretrained weights.
root : str, default '~/.mxnet/models'
    Location for keeping the model parameters.

Returns
-------
mxnet.gluon.HybridBlock
    Hybrid faster RCNN network.

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.inception.inception_v3
documentation:
Inception v3 model from
["Rethinking the Inception Architecture for Computer Vision"](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.00567) paper.

# Parameters

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

partial_bn

: Freeze all batch normalization layers during training except the first layer.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.
Constructor

classes.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.alpha_pose.utils.ZeroUniform
documentation:
Initializes weights with random values uniformly sampled from a given range.

# Parameters

scale

: The bound on the range of the generated random values.
  Values are generated from the range \[0, `scale`\].
  Default scale is 1.

# Example

```pycon
>>> # Given 'module', an instance of 'mxnet.module.Module', initialize weights
>>> # to random values uniformly sampled between 0 and 0.1.
...
>>> init = ZeroUniform(0.1)
>>> module.init_params(init)
>>> for dictionary in module.get_params():
...     for key in dictionary:
...         print(key)
...         print(dictionary[key].asnumpy())
...
fullyconnected0_weight
[[ 0.01360891 0.02144304  0.08511933]]
```

documentation:
Constructs a ResNetV1b-18 model.

# Parameters

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

dilated: bool, default False

: Whether to apply dilation strategy to ResNetV1b, yielding a stride 8 model.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

last_gamma

: Whether to initialize the gamma of the last BatchNorm layer in each bottleneck to zero.

use_global_stats

: Whether forcing BatchNorm to use global statistics instead of minibatch statistics;
  optionally set to True if finetuning using ImageNet classification pretrained models.

methods.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.resnetv1b.ResNetV1b.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=unused-variable

Pre-trained ResNetV1b Model, which produces the strides of 8
featuremaps at conv5.

# Parameters

block

: Class for the residual block. Options are BasicBlockV1, BottleneckV1.

layers

: Numbers of layers in each block

classes

: Number of classification classes.

dilated

: Applying dilation strategy to pretrained ResNet yielding a stride-8 model,
  typically used in Semantic Segmentation.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

last_gamma

: Whether to initialize the gamma of the last BatchNorm layer in each bottleneck to zero.

deep_stem

: Whether to replace the 7x7 conv1 with 3 3x3 convolution layers.

avg_down

: Whether to use average pooling for projection skip connection between stages/downsample.

final_drop

: Dropout ratio before the final classification layer.

use_global_stats

: Whether forcing BatchNorm to use global statistics instead of minibatch statistics;
  optionally set to True if finetuning using ImageNet classification pretrained models.

Reference:

> - He, Kaiming, et al. "Deep residual learning for image recognition."
>
> Proceedings of the IEEE conference on computer vision and pattern recognition. 2016.
>
> - Yu, Fisher, and Vladlen Koltun. "Multi-scale context aggregation by dilated convolutions."

functions.name: gluoncv.model_zoo.mobilenetv3.get_mobilenet_v3
documentation:
MobileNet model from the
["Searching for MobileNetV3"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244) paper.

# Parameters

model_name

: The name of mobilenetv3 models, large and small are supported.

multiplier

: The width multiplier for controlling the model size. Only multipliers that are no
  less than 0.25 are supported. The actual number of channels is equal to the original
  channel size multiplied by this multiplier.

pretrained

: Boolean value controls whether to load the default pretrained weights for model.
  String value represents the hashtag for a certain version of pretrained weights.

ctx

: The context in which to load the pretrained weights.

root

: Location for keeping the model parameters.

norm_layer

: Normalization layer used (default: {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm`)
  Can be {class}`mxnet.gluon.nn.BatchNorm` or {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

norm_kwargs

: Additional `norm_layer` arguments, for example `num_devices=4`
  for {class}`mxnet.gluon.contrib.nn.SyncBatchNorm`.

documentation: Pascal VOC Semantic Segmentation Dataset.

classes.name: gluoncv.data.pascal_voc.segmentation.VOCSegmentation

methods.name: gluoncv.data.pascal_voc.segmentation.VOCSegmentation.classes
documentation: Category names.

Pascal VOC Semantic Segmentation Dataset.

# Parameters

root

: Path to VOCdevkit folder. Default is '\$(HOME)/mxnet/datasets/voc'

split: string

: 'train', 'val' or 'test'

transform

: A function that transforms the image

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from mxnet.gluon.data.vision import transforms
>>> # Transforms for Normalization
>>> input_transform = transforms.Compose([
>>>     transforms.ToTensor(),
>>>     transforms.Normalize([.485, .456, .406], [.229, .224, .225]),
>>> ])
>>> # Create Dataset
>>> trainset = gluoncv.data.VOCSegmentation(split='train', transform=input_transform)
>>> # Create Training Loader
>>> train_data = gluon.data.DataLoader(
>>>     trainset, 4, shuffle=True, last_batch='rollover',
>>>     num_workers=4)
```

methods.name: gluoncv.data.pascal_voc.detection.VOCDetection._preload_labels
documentation: Preload all labels into memory.

Pascal VOC detection Dataset.

# Parameters

root

: Path to folder storing the dataset.

splits

: List of combinations of (year, name)
  For years, candidates can be: 2007, 2012.
  For names, candidates can be: 'train', 'val', 'trainval', 'test'.

transform

: A function that takes data and label and transforms them. Refer to
  {doc}`./transforms` for examples.

  A transform function for object detection should take label into consideration,
  because any geometric modification will require label to be modified.

index_map

: In default, the 20 classes are mapped into indices from 0 to 19. We can
  customize it by providing a str to int dict specifying how to map class
  names to indices. Use by advanced users only, when you want to swap the orders
  of class labels.

preload_label

: If True, then parse and load all labels into memory during
  initialization. It often accelerate speed but require more memory
  usage. Typical preloaded labels took tens of MB. You only need to disable it
  when your dataset is extremely large.

Custom Pascal VOC detection Dataset.

    If True, generate class labels base on the annotations instead of the default classe labels.

documentation: Parse xml file and return labels.

Base class for custom Dataset which follows protocol/formatting of the well-known VOC object detection dataset.

# Parameters

class: tuple of classes, default = None

: We reuse the neural network weights if the corresponding class appears in the pretrained model.
  Otherwise, we randomly initialize the neural network weights for new classes.

root

: Path to folder storing the dataset.

splits

: List of combinations of (year, name)
  For years, candidates can be: 2007, 2012.
  For names, candidates can be: 'train', 'val', 'trainval', 'test'.

transform

: A function that takes data and label and transforms them. Refer to
  {doc}`./transforms` for examples.
  A transform function for object detection should take label into consideration,
  because any geometric modification will require label to be modified.

index_map

: By default, the 20 classes are mapped into indices from 0 to 19. We can
  customize it by providing a str to int dict specifying how to map class
  names to indices. This is only for advanced users, when you want to swap the orders
  of class labels.

preload_label

: If True, then parse and load all labels into memory during
  initialization. It often accelerate speed but require more memory
  usage. Typical preloaded labels took tens of MB. You only need to disable it
  when your dataset is extremely large.

methods.name: gluoncv.data.mscoco.keypoints.COCOKeyPoints._get_keypoints_center_count
documentation: Get geometric center of all keypoints

COCO keypoint detection dataset.

Parameters
----------
root : str, default '~/mxnet/datasets/coco'
    Path to folder storing the dataset.
splits : list of str, default ['person_keypoints_val2017']
    Json annotations name.
    Candidates can be: person_keypoints_val2017, person_keypoints_train2017.
check_centers : bool, default is False
    If true, will force check centers of bbox and keypoints, respectively.
    If centers are far away from each other, remove this label.
skip_empty : bool, default is False
    Whether skip entire image if no valid label is found. Use `False` if this dataset is
    for validation to avoid COCO metric error.

methods.name: gluoncv.data.mscoco.segmentation.COCOSegmentation.classes
documentation: Category names.

COCO Semantic Segmentation Dataset for VOC Pre-training.

# Parameters

root

: Path to COCO folder. Default is '\$(HOME)/mxnet/datasets/coco'

split: string

: 'train', 'val' or 'test'

transform

: A function that transforms the image

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from mxnet.gluon.data.vision import transforms
>>> # Transforms for Normalization
>>> input_transform = transforms.Compose([
>>>     transforms.ToTensor(),
>>>     transforms.Normalize([.485, .456, .406], [.229, .224, .225]),
>>> ])
>>> # Create Dataset
>>> trainset = gluoncv.data.COCOSegmentation(split='train', transform=input_transform)
>>> # Create Training Loader
>>> train_data = gluon.data.DataLoader(
>>>     trainset, 4, shuffle=True, last_batch='rollover',
>>>     num_workers=4)
```

MS COCO detection dataset.

# Parameters

root

: Path to folder storing the dataset.

splits

: Json annotations name.
  Candidates can be: instances_val2017, instances_train2017.

transform

: A function that takes data and label and transforms them. Refer to
  {doc}`./transforms` for examples.

  A transform function for object detection should take label into consideration,
  because any geometric modification will require label to be modified.

min_object_area

: Minimum accepted ground-truth area, if an object's area is smaller than this value,
  it will be ignored.

skip_empty

: Whether skip images with no valid object. This should be `True` in training, otherwise
  it will cause undefined behavior.

use_crowd

: Whether use boxes labeled as crowd instance.

documentation: Category names.

ADE20K Semantic Segmentation Dataset.

# Parameters

root

: Path to VOCdevkit folder. Default is '\$(HOME)/mxnet/datasplits/ade'

split: string

: 'train', 'val' or 'test'

transform

: A function that transforms the image

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from mxnet.gluon.data.vision import transforms
>>> # Transforms for Normalization
>>> input_transform = transforms.Compose([
>>>     transforms.ToTensor(),
>>>     transforms.Normalize([.485, .456, .406], [.229, .224, .225]),
>>> ])
>>> # Create Dataset
>>> trainset = gluoncv.data.ADE20KSegmentation(split='train', transform=input_transform)
>>> # Create Training Loader
>>> train_data = gluon.data.DataLoader(
>>>     trainset, 4, shuffle=True, last_batch='rollover',
>>>     num_workers=4)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.data.recordio.detection.RecordFileDetection
documentation:
Detection dataset loaded from record file.
The supported record file is using the same format used by
{py:meth}`mxnet.image.ImageDetIter` and {py:meth}`mxnet.io.ImageDetRecordIter`.

Checkout {ref}`lst_record_dataset` for tutorial of how to prepare this file.

:::{note}
We suggest you to use `RecordFileDetection` only if you are familiar with
the record files.
:::

# Parameters

filename

: Path of the record file. It require both .rec and .idx file in the same
  directory, where raw image and labels are stored in .rec file for better
  IO performance, .idx file is used to provide random access to the binary file.

coord_normalized

: Indicate whether bounding box coordinates have been normalized to (0, 1) in labels.
  If so, we will rescale back to absolute coordinates by multiplying width or height.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> record_dataset = RecordFileDetection('train.rec')
>>> img, label = record_dataset[0]
>>> print(img.shape, label.shape)
(512, 512, 3) (1, 5)
```

classes.name: gluoncv.data.kinetics400.classification.Kinetics400
documentation:
Load the Kinetics400 video action recognition dataset.

Refer to {doc}`../build/examples_datasets/kinetics400` for the description of
this dataset and how to prepare it.

# Parameters

root

: Path to the root folder storing the dataset.

setting

: A text file describing the dataset, each line per video sample.
  There are three items in each line: (1) video path; (2) video length and (3) video label.

train

: Whether to load the training or validation set.

test_mode

: Whether to perform evaluation on the test set.
  Usually there is three-crop or ten-crop evaluation strategy involved.

name_pattern

: The naming pattern of the decoded video frames.
  For example, img_00012.jpg.

video_ext

: If video_loader is set to True, please specify the video format accordinly.

is_color

: Whether the loaded image is color or grayscale.

modality

: Input modalities, we support only rgb video frames for now.
  Will add support for rgb difference image and optical flow image later.

num_segments

: Number of segments to evenly divide the video into clips.
  A useful technique to obtain global video-level information.
  Limin Wang, etal, Temporal Segment Networks: Towards Good Practices for Deep Action Recognition, ECCV 2016.

num_crop

: Number of crops for each image. default is 1.
  Common choices are three crops and ten crops during evaluation.

new_length

: The length of input video clip. Default is a single image, but it can be multiple video frames.
  For example, new_length=16 means we will extract a video clip of consecutive 16 frames.

new_step

: Temporal sampling rate. For example, new_step=1 means we will extract a video clip of consecutive frames.
  new_step=2 means we will extract a video clip of every other frame.

new_width

: Scale the width of loaded image to 'new_width' for later multiscale cropping and resizing.

new_height

: Scale the height of loaded image to 'new_height' for later multiscale cropping and resizing.

target_width

: Scale the width of transformed image to the same 'target_width' for batch forwarding.

target_height

: Scale the height of transformed image to the same 'target_height' for batch forwarding.

temporal_jitter

: Whether to temporally jitter if new_step > 1.

video_loader

: Whether to use video loader to load data.

use_decord

: Whether to use Decord video loader to load data. Otherwise use mmcv video loader.

transform

: A function that takes data and label and transforms them.

slowfast

: If set to True, use data loader designed for SlowFast network.
  Christoph Feichtenhofer, etal, SlowFast Networks for Video Recognition, ICCV 2019.

slow_temporal_stride

: The temporal stride for sparse sampling of video frames in slow branch of a SlowFast network.

fast_temporal_stride

: The temporal stride for sparse sampling of video frames in fast branch of a SlowFast network.

data_aug

: Different types of data augmentation auto. Supports v1, v2, v3 and v4.

lazy_init

: If set to True, build a dataset instance without loading any dataset.

classes.name: gluoncv.data.dataloader.DetectionDataLoader
documentation:
Data loader for detection dataset.

:::{deprecated} 0.2.0 :py:class:`DetectionDataLoader` is deprecated, please use :py:class:`mxnet.gluon.data.DataLoader` with batchify functions listed in `gluoncv.data.batchify` directly.
:::

It loads data batches from a dataset and then apply data
transformations. It's a subclass of {py:class}`mxnet.gluon.data.DataLoader`,
and therefore has very similar APIs.

The main purpose of the DataLoader is to pad variable length of labels from
each image, because they have different amount of objects.

# Parameters

dataset

: The source dataset.

batch_size

: The size of mini-batch.

shuffle

: If or not randomly shuffle the samples. Often use True for training
  dataset and False for validation/test datasets

sampler

: The sampler to use. We should either specify a sampler or enable
  shuffle, not both, because random shuffling is a sampling method.

last_batch

: How to handle the last batch if the batch size does not evenly divide by
  the number of examples in the dataset. There are three options to deal
  with the last batch if its size is smaller than the specified batch
  size.

  - keep: keep it
  - discard: throw it away
  - rollover: insert the examples to the beginning of the next batch

batch_sampler

: A sampler that returns mini-batches. Do not specify batch_size,
  shuffle, sampler, and last_batch if batch_sampler is specified.

batchify_fn

: Callback function to allow users to specify how to merge samples
  into a batch.
  Defaults to {py:meth}`gluoncv.data.dataloader.default_pad_batchify_fn`:

  ```
  def default_pad_batchify_fn(data):
      if isinstance(data[0], nd.NDArray):
          return nd.stack(*data)
      elif isinstance(data[0], tuple):
          data = zip(*data)
          return [pad_batchify(i) for i in data]
      else:
          data = np.asarray(data)
          pad = max([l.shape[0] for l in data])
          buf = np.full((len(data), pad, data[0].shape[-1]),
                        -1, dtype=data[0].dtype)
          for i, l in enumerate(data):
              buf[i][:l.shape[0], :] = l
          return nd.array(buf, dtype=data[0].dtype)
  ```

num_workers

: The number of multiprocessing workers to use for data preprocessing.
  If `num_workers` = 0, multiprocessing is disabled.
  Otherwise `num_workers` multiprocessing worker is used to process data.

Internal multi-worker iterator for DataLoader.

classes.name: gluoncv.data.dataloader.RandomTransformDataLoader
documentation:
DataLoader that support random transform function applied to dataset.

# Parameters

transform_fns

: Transform functions that takes a sample as input and returns the transformed sample.
  They will be randomly selected during the dataloader iteration.

dataset

: The source dataset. Original dataset is recommended here since we will apply transform
  function from candidates again during the iteration.

interval

: For every `interval` batches, transform function is randomly selected from candidates.

batch_size

: The size of mini-batch.

shuffle

: If or not randomly shuffle the samples. Often use True for training
  dataset and False for validation/test datasets

sampler

: The sampler to use. We should either specify a sampler or enable
  shuffle, not both, because random shuffling is a sampling method.

last_batch

: How to handle the last batch if the batch size does not evenly divide by
  the number of examples in the dataset. There are three options to deal
  with the last batch if its size is smaller than the specified batch
  size.

  - keep: keep it
  - discard: throw it away
  - rollover: insert the examples to the beginning of the next batch

batch_sampler

: A sampler that returns mini-batches. Do not specify batch_size,
  shuffle, sampler, and last_batch if batch_sampler is specified.

batchify_fn

: Callback function to allow users to specify how to merge samples
  into a batch.
  Defaults to {py:meth}`gluoncv.data.dataloader.default_pad_batchify_fn`:

  ```
  def default_pad_batchify_fn(data):
      if isinstance(data[0], nd.NDArray):
          return nd.stack(*data)
      elif isinstance(data[0], tuple):
          data = zip(*data)
          return [pad_batchify(i) for i in data]
      else:
          data = np.asarray(data)
          pad = max([l.shape[0] for l in data])
          buf = np.full((len(data), pad, data[0].shape[-1]),
                        -1, dtype=data[0].dtype)
          for i, l in enumerate(data):
              buf[i][:l.shape[0], :] = l
          return nd.array(buf, dtype=data[0].dtype)
  ```

num_workers

: The number of multiprocessing workers to use for data preprocessing.
  If `num_workers` = 0, multiprocessing is disabled.
  Otherwise `num_workers` multiprocessing worker is used to process data.

pin_memory

: If `True`, the dataloader will copy NDArrays into pinned memory
  before returning them. Copying from CPU pinned memory to GPU is faster
  than from normal CPU memory.

pin_device_id

: The device id to use for allocating pinned memory if pin_memory is `True`

prefetch

: The number of prefetching batches only works if `num_workers` > 0.
  If `prefetch` > 0, it allow worker process to prefetch certain batches before
  acquiring data from iterators.
  Note that using large prefetching batch will provide smoother bootstrapping performance,
  but will consume more shared_memory. Using smaller number may forfeit the purpose of using
  multiple worker processes, try reduce `num_workers` in this case.
  By default it defaults to `num_workers * 2`.

documentation:
Load your own video classification dataset.

Parameters
----------
root : str, required.
    Path to the root folder storing the dataset.
setting : str, required.
    A text file describing the dataset, each line per video sample.
    There are three items in each line: (1) video path; (2) video length and (3) video label.
train : bool, default True.
    Whether to load the training or validation set.
test_mode : bool, default False.
    Whether to perform evaluation on the test set.
    Usually there is three-crop or ten-crop evaluation strategy involved.
name_pattern : str, default None.
    The naming pattern of the decoded video frames.
    For example, img_00012.jpg.
video_ext : str, default 'mp4'.
    If video_loader is set to True, please specify the video format accordinly.
is_color : bool, default True.
    Whether the loaded image is color or grayscale.
modality : str, default 'rgb'.
    Input modalities, we support only rgb video frames for now.
    Will add support for rgb difference image and optical flow image later.
num_segments : int, default 1.
    Number of segments to evenly divide the video into clips.
    A useful technique to obtain global video-level information.
    Limin Wang, etal, Temporal Segment Networks: Towards Good Practices for Deep Action Recognition, ECCV 2016.
num_crop : int, default 1.
    Number of crops for each image. default is 1.
    Common choices are three crops and ten crops during evaluation.
new_length : int, default 1.
    The length of input video clip. Default is a single image, but it can be multiple video frames.
    For example, new_length=16 means we will extract a video clip of consecutive 16 frames.
new_step : int, default 1.
    Temporal sampling rate. For example, new_step=1 means we will extract a video clip of consecutive frames.
    new_step=2 means we will extract a video clip of every other frame.
new_width : int, default 340.
    Scale the width of loaded image to 'new_width' for later multiscale cropping and resizing.
new_height : int, default 256.
    Scale the height of loaded image to 'new_height' for later multiscale cropping and resizing.
target_width : int, default 224.
    Scale the width of transformed image to the same 'target_width' for batch forwarding.
target_height : int, default 224.
    Scale the height of transformed image to the same 'target_height' for batch forwarding.
temporal_jitter : bool, default False.
    Whether to temporally jitter if new_step > 1.
video_loader : bool, default False.
    Whether to use video loader to load data.
use_decord : bool, default True.
    Whether to use Decord video loader to load data. Otherwise use mmcv video loader.
transform : function, default None.
    A function that takes data and label and transforms them.
slowfast : bool, default False.
    If set to True, use data loader designed for SlowFast network.
    Christoph Feichtenhofer, etal, SlowFast Networks for Video Recognition, ICCV 2019.
slow_temporal_stride : int, default 16.
    The temporal stride for sparse sampling of video frames in slow branch of a SlowFast network.
fast_temporal_stride : int, default 2.
    The temporal stride for sparse sampling of video frames in fast branch of a SlowFast network.
data_aug : str, default 'v1'.
    Different types of data augmentation auto. Supports v1, v2, v3 and v4.
lazy_init : bool, default False.
    If set to True, build a dataset instance without loading any dataset.

> Path to KITTI RAW dataset folder. Default is '\$(HOME)/.mxnet/datasets/kitti/kitti_data'

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gluoncv.data.kitti.kitti_utils import dict_batchify_fn, readlines
>>> train_filenames = os.path.join(
>>>     os.path.expanduser("~"), '/.mxnet/datasets/kitti/splits/eigen_full/train_files.txt')
>>> train_filenames = readlines(train_filenames)
>>> # Create Dataset
>>> trainset = gluoncv.data.KITTIRAWDataset(
>>>         filenames=train_filenames, height=192, width=640,
>>>         frame_idxs=[0], num_scales=4, is_train=True, img_ext='.png')
>>> # Create Training Loader
>>> train_data = gluon.data.DataLoader(
>>>     trainset, batch_size=12, shuffle=True,
>>>     batchify_fn=dict_batchify_fn, num_workers=12,
>>>     pin_memory=True, last_batch='discard')
```

Resize colour images to the required scales and augment if required

We create the color_aug object in advance and apply the same augmentation to all
images in this item. This ensures that all images input to the pose network receive the
same augmentation.

methods.name: gluoncv.data.kitti.mono_dataset.MonoDataset.__getitem__
documentation:
Returns a single training item from the dataset as a dictionary.

Values correspond to mxnet NDArray.
Keys in the dictionary are either strings or tuples:

    ("color", <frame_id>, <scale>)          for raw colour images,
    ("color_aug", <frame_id>, <scale>)      for augmented colour images,
    ("K", scale) or ("inv_K", scale)        for camera intrinsics,
    "stereo_T"                              for camera extrinsics, and
    "depth_gt"                              for ground truth depth maps.

<frame_id> is either:
    an integer (e.g. 0, -1, or 1) representing the temporal step relative to 'index',
or
    "s" for the opposite image in the stereo pair.

<scale> is an integer representing the scale of the image relative to the full-size image:
    -1      images at native resolution as loaded from disk
    0       images resized to (self.width,      self.height     )
    1       images resized to (self.width // 2, self.height // 2)
    2       images resized to (self.width // 4, self.height // 4)
    3       images resized to (self.width // 8, self.height // 8)

Superclass for monocular dataloaders
Parameters
----------
data_path : string
    Path to dataset folder.
filenames : string
    Path to split file.
    For example: '$(HOME)/.mxnet/datasets/kitti/splits/eigen_full/train_files.txt'
height : int
    The height for input images.
width : int
    The height for input images.
frame_idxs : list
    The frames to load.
    an integer (e.g. 0, -1, or 1) representing the temporal step relative to 'index',
    or "s" for the opposite image in the stereo pair.
num_scales : int
    The number of scales of the image relative to the full-size image.
is_train : bool
    Whether use Data Augmentation. Default is: False
img_ext : string
    The extension name of input image. Default is '.jpg'
